message.content: A minha feature já está inserida no remote config, paga que eu possa desliga-la, https://console.firebase.google.com/project/fittoday-2aaff/config/env/firebase, como ela é exibida para o usuário? somente se tiver um desafio vinculado ou criado?

content.text: Let me investigate how the social feed feature is currently displayed.

input.prompt:
Find how the social feed feature is displayed to the user in this iOS app. I need to understand:
1. Where is the social feed tab/entry point in the UI?
2. Is it gated behind any conditions (like having a challenge)?
3. How does the Remote Config feature flag work for this feature?
4. What determines visibility of the social feed?

Search thoroughly in the Presentation layer, look for social feed views, view models, tab views, and any feature flag/remote config integration. Also check for any challenge-related conditions.

    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Key
    12→
    13→/// Keys for remote feature flags controlled via Firebase Remote Config.
    14→/// Each key corresponds to a boolean flag that can be toggled remotely.
    15→enum FeatureFlagKey: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - AI Features
    18→
    19→    /// Enables AI-powered workout generation (OpenAI).
    20→    case aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled = "ai_workout_generation_enabled"
    21→
    22→    /// Enables AI exercise substitution during active workout.
    23→    case aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled = "ai_exercise_substitution_enabled"
    24→
    25→    // MARK: - Social Features
    26→
    27→    /// Enables group creation, joining, and management.
    28→    case socialGroupsEnabled = "social_groups_enabled"
    29→
    30→    /// Enables workout challenges within groups.
    31→    case challengesEnabled = "challenges_enabled"
    32→
    33→    /// Enables leaderboard ranking in groups.
    34→    case leaderboardEnabled = "leaderboard_enabled"
    35→
    36→    /// Enables photo check-in for challenge validation.
    37→    case checkInEnabled = "check_in_enabled"
    38→

    42→    /// Enables social feed with workout proof posts.

    44→
    45→    // MARK: - Health & Sync
    46→

    48→    case healthKitSyncEnabled = "healthkit_sync_enabled"
    49→
    50→    /// Enables activity stats with Swift Charts.

    52→
    53→    // MARK: - Content

    55→    /// Enables structured training programs.
    56→    case programsEnabled = "programs_enabled"
    57→
    58→    /// Enables user-created workout templates.
    59→    case customWorkoutsEnabled = "custom_workouts_enabled"
    60→
    61→    /// Enables exercise library/explorer.
    62→    case exerciseLibraryEnabled = "exercise_library_enabled"
    63→
    64→    // MARK: - Personal Trainer
    65→
    66→    /// Enables personal trainer integration features.
    67→    /// When enabled, users can connect with personal trainers via CMS.
    68→    case personalTrainerEnabled = "personal_trainer_enabled"
    69→
    70→    /// Enables synchronization of workouts from CMS.
    71→    /// When enabled, trainer-assigned workouts sync to the user's app.
    72→    case cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = "cms_workout_sync_enabled"

    76→    case trainerChatEnabled = "trainer_chat_enabled"

    85→
    86→    // MARK: - Operational

    88→    /// Shows maintenance banner and disables write operations.
    89→    case maintenanceModeEnabled = "maintenance_mode_enabled"
    90→
    91→    /// Shows non-dismissable update dialog directing to App Store.

    93→
    94→    // MARK: - Display Name

    96→    /// Human-readable display name for the feature flag.
    97→    var displayName: String {
    98→        switch self {

   102→        // Social
   103→        case .socialGroupsEnabled: return "Social Groups"
   104→        case .challengesEnabled: return "Challenges"

   111→        case .statsChartsEnabled: return "Stats Charts"
   112→        // Content
   113→        case .programsEnabled: return "Training Programs"
   114→        case .customWorkoutsEnabled: return "Custom Workouts"

   116→        // Personal Trainer
   117→        case .personalTrainerEnabled: return "Personal Trainer Integration"
   118→        case .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled: return "CMS Workout Sync"
   119→        case .trainerChatEnabled: return "Trainer Chat"

   134→    var defaultValue: Bool {

     4→//

     8→import Foundation
     9→import SwiftUI
    10→
    11→enum AppTab: Hashable, CaseIterable {
    12→    case home
    13→    case workout
    14→    case fitorb
    15→    case activity
    16→    case profile
    17→
    18→    var title: String {
    19→        switch self {
    20→        case .home: return "tab.home".localized
    21→        case .workout: return "tab.workout".localized
    22→        case .fitorb: return "tab.fitorb".localized
    23→        case .activity: return "tab.activity".localized
    24→        case .profile: return "tab.profile".localized
    25→        }
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    var systemImage: String {
    29→        switch self {
    30→        case .home: return "house.fill"
    31→        case .workout: return "dumbbell.fill"
    32→        case .fitorb: return "sparkles"
    33→        case .activity: return "chart.bar.fill"
    34→        case .profile: return "person.fill"
    35→        }
    36→    }
    37→}
    38→
    39→enum AppRoute: Hashable {
    40→    case onboarding
    41→    case editProfile  // Rota separada para edição de perfil existente
    42→    case setup
    43→    case workoutPlan(UUID)
    44→    case exerciseDetail
    45→    case workoutExecution  // Nova view de execução com timer e pause/play
    46→    case workoutExercisePreview(ExercisePrescription)  // Preview de exercício (não altera índice)
    47→    case workoutSummary
    48→    case paywall
    49→    case programDetail(String)  // Detalhe do programa
    50→    case programWorkoutDetail(ProgramWorkout)  // Detalhe de um treino dentro do programa
    51→    case libraryWorkoutDetail(String)  // Detalhe de um treino da biblioteca (por ID)
    52→    case programExerciseDetail(ExercisePrescription)  // Detalhe de exercício na biblioteca/programa
    53→    case apiKeySettings  // Configuração de chave de API do usuário
    54→    case healthKitSettings // Integração Apple Health (PRO)
    55→    case privacySettings  // Privacy settings for social features
    56→    case authentication(inviteContext: String?)  // Authentication flow
    57→    case groupInvite(groupId: String)  // Group invitation deep link
    58→    case notifications  // Notifications screen for challenges
    59→    case customWorkouts  // Custom workout templates list
    60→    case customWorkoutBuilder(UUID?)  // Create or edit custom workout (nil = new)
    61→    case personalTrainer  // Personal Trainer connection and workouts
    62→    case trainerSearch  // Search for personal trainers
    63→    case trainerDashboard  // Trainer dashboard (tabs: Today, Chat, History)
    64→    case trainerChat  // Direct access to trainer chat tab
    65→    case cmsWorkoutDetail(String)  // CMS workout detail by ID
    66→    case cmsWorkoutFeedback(String)  // CMS workout feedback by ID
    67→    case aiChat  // AI Fitness Assistant (FitOrb)
    68→    case socialFeed  // Social feed with workout proofs
    69→}
    70→
    71→struct DeepLink {
    72→    enum Destination {
    73→        case home
    74→        case onboarding
    75→        case setup
    76→        case paywall
    77→        case groupInvite(groupId: String)
    78→        case trainerDashboard
    79→        case trainerChat
    80→        case workoutExecution
    81→    }
    82→
    83→    let destination: Destination
    84→
    85→    init?(url: URL) {
    86→        guard let scheme = url.scheme?.lowercased(), scheme == "fittoday" else {
    87→            return nil
    88→        }
    89→
    90→        let host = url.host?.lowercased() ?? ""
    91→        let path = url.path.lowercased()
    92→
    93→        // Handle group invite: fittoday://group/invite/{groupId}
    94→        if host == "group", path.hasPrefix("/invite/") {
    95→            let groupId = String(path.dropFirst("/invite/".count))
    96→            destination = .groupInvite(groupId: groupId)
    97→            return
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        // Handle workout routes: fittoday://workout/execution
   101→        if host == "workout", path == "/execution" {
   102→            destination = .workoutExecution
   103→            return
   104→        }
   105→
   106→        // Handle trainer routes: fittoday://trainer/dashboard, fittoday://trainer/chat
   107→        if host == "trainer" {
   108→            switch path.lowercased() {
   109→            case "/dashboard":
   110→                destination = .trainerDashboard
   111→                return
   112→            case "/chat":
   113→                destination = .trainerChat
   114→                return
   115→            default:
   116→                break
   117→            }
   118→        }
   119→
   120→        // Handle other routes
   121→        let pathOrHost = !host.isEmpty ? host : path
   122→        switch pathOrHost {
   123→        case "home", "/home":
   124→            destination = .home
   125→        case "onboarding", "/onboarding":
   126→            destination = .onboarding
   127→        case "setup", "/setup":
   128→            destination = .setup
   129→        case "paywall", "/paywall":
   130→            destination = .paywall
   131→        default:
   132→            return nil
   133→        }
   134→    }
   135→}
   136→
   137→@MainActor
   138→protocol AppRouting: AnyObject {
   139→    var selectedTab: AppTab { get set }
   140→    var tabPaths: [AppTab: NavigationPath] { get set }
   141→
   142→    func select(tab: AppTab)
   143→    func push(_ route: AppRoute, on tab: AppTab?)
   144→    func pop(on tab: AppTab?)
   145→    func handle(deeplink: DeepLink)
   146→}
   147→
   148→// 💡 Learn: Navegação com @Observable permite melhor performance
   149→@MainActor
   150→@Observable final class AppRouter: AppRouting {
   151→    var selectedTab: AppTab = .home
   152→    var tabPaths: [AppTab: NavigationPath] = [:]
   153→
   154→    init() {
   155→        AppTab.allCases.forEach { tab in
   156→            tabPaths[tab] = NavigationPath()
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    func select(tab: AppTab) {
   161→        selectedTab = tab
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    func push(_ route: AppRoute, on tab: AppTab? = nil) {
   165→        let target = tab ?? selectedTab
   166→        if tabPaths[target] == nil {
   167→            tabPaths[target] = NavigationPath()
   168→        }
   169→        tabPaths[target]?.append(route)
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    func pop(on tab: AppTab? = nil) {
   173→        let target = tab ?? selectedTab
   174→        guard var path = tabPaths[target], !path.isEmpty else {
   175→            return
   176→        }
   177→        path.removeLast()
   178→        tabPaths[target] = path
   179→    }
   180→
   181→    func handle(deeplink: DeepLink) {
   182→        switch deeplink.destination {
   183→        case .home:
   184→            select(tab: .home)
   185→        case .onboarding:
   186→            select(tab: .home)
   187→            push(.onboarding, on: .home)
   188→        case .setup:
   189→            select(tab: .home)
   190→            push(.setup, on: .home)
   191→        case .paywall:
   192→            select(tab: .home)
   193→            push(.paywall, on: .home)
   194→        case .groupInvite(let groupId):
   195→            select(tab: .home)
   196→            push(.groupInvite(groupId: groupId), on: .home)
   197→        case .trainerDashboard:
   198→            select(tab: .home)
   199→            push(.trainerDashboard, on: .home)
   200→        case .trainerChat:
   201→            select(tab: .home)
   202→            push(.trainerChat, on: .home)
   203→        case .workoutExecution:
   204→            push(.workoutExecution, on: selectedTab)
   205→        }
   206→    }
   207→
   208→    func handle(url: URL) {
   209→        guard let link = DeepLink(url: url) else { return }
   210→        handle(deeplink: link)
   211→    }
   212→}
   213→
   214→

1→//
     2→//  RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Implementation of FeatureFlagRepository using Firebase Remote Config.
     6→//  Includes UserDefaults caching for offline support.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Remote Config Feature Flag Repository
    12→
    13→/// Implementation of FeatureFlagRepository that uses Firebase Remote Config
    14→/// with UserDefaults caching for offline access.
    15→final class RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository: FeatureFlagRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
    16→    // MARK: - Properties
    17→
    18→    private let remoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing
    19→    private let userDefaults: UserDefaults
    20→    private let cacheKeyPrefix = "feature_flag_cache_"
    21→
    22→    // MARK: - Initialization
    23→
    24→    /// Creates a new feature flag repository.
    25→    /// - Parameters:
    26→    ///   - remoteConfigService: The remote config service to use for fetching values.
    27→    ///   - userDefaults: UserDefaults instance for caching (defaults to standard).
    28→    init(
    29→        remoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing,
    30→        userDefaults: UserDefaults = .standard
    31→    ) {
    32→        self.remoteConfigService = remoteConfigService
    33→        self.userDefaults = userDefaults
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    // MARK: - FeatureFlagRepository
    37→
    38→    func isEnabled(_ key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
    39→        // Get value from remote config service
    40→        let remoteValue = await remoteConfigService.getValue(for: key)
    41→
    42→        // Cache the value for offline access
    43→        cacheValue(remoteValue, for: key)
    44→
    45→        return remoteValue
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws {
    49→        try await remoteConfigService.fetchAndActivate()
    50→
    51→        // After successful fetch, update cache for all keys
    52→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    53→            let value = await remoteConfigService.getValue(for: key)
    54→            cacheValue(value, for: key)
    55→        }
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    // MARK: - Caching
    59→
    60→    /// Caches a feature flag value to UserDefaults.
    61→    /// - Parameters:
    62→    ///   - value: The boolean value to cache.
    63→    ///   - key: The feature flag key.
    64→    private func cacheValue(_ value: Bool, for key: FeatureFlagKey) {
    65→        let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    66→        userDefaults.set(value, forKey: cacheKey)
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    /// Retrieves a cached feature flag value from UserDefaults.
    70→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key.
    71→    /// - Returns: The cached value, or the default value if not cached.
    72→    func getCachedValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) -> Bool {
    73→        let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    74→
    75→        // Check if value exists in cache
    76→        if userDefaults.object(forKey: cacheKey) != nil {
    77→            return userDefaults.bool(forKey: cacheKey)
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        // Return default value if not cached
    81→        return key.defaultValue
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    /// Clears all cached feature flag values.
    85→    func clearCache() {
    86→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    87→            let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    88→            userDefaults.removeObject(forKey: cacheKey)
    89→        }
    90→    }
    91→}

1→//
     2→//  FeatureFlagUseCase.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Use case for checking feature flags with entitlement integration.
     6→//  Combines remote feature flags with subscription-based access control.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Checking Protocol
    12→
    13→/// Protocol for checking feature flag states with entitlement integration.
    14→protocol FeatureFlagChecking: Sendable {
    15→    /// Checks if a feature flag is enabled.
    16→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key to check.
    17→    /// - Returns: `true` if the feature is enabled, `false` otherwise.
    18→    func isFeatureEnabled(_ key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool
    19→
    20→    /// Checks feature access combining feature flag and entitlement.
    21→    /// - Parameters:
    22→    ///   - feature: The Pro feature to check access for.
    23→    ///   - flag: The feature flag that must be enabled.
    24→    /// - Returns: The result of the access check.
    25→    func checkFeatureAccess(_ feature: ProFeature, flag: FeatureFlagKey) async -> FeatureAccessResult
    26→
    27→    /// Refreshes feature flags from remote config.
    28→    /// - Throws: Error if refresh fails.
    29→    func refreshFlags() async throws
    30→}
    31→
    32→// MARK: - Feature Flag Use Case
    33→
    34→/// Use case implementation for feature flag checking.
    35→/// Logic: Check flag first, then delegate to entitlement if enabled.
    36→final class FeatureFlagUseCase: FeatureFlagChecking, @unchecked Sendable {
    37→    // MARK: - Properties
    38→
    39→    private let featureFlagRepository: FeatureFlagRepository
    40→    private let featureGating: FeatureGating
    41→
    42→    // MARK: - Initialization
    43→
    44→    /// Creates a new feature flag use case.
    45→    /// - Parameters:
    46→    ///   - featureFlagRepository: Repository for feature flag access.
    47→    ///   - featureGating: Use case for entitlement-based feature gating.
    48→    init(
    49→        featureFlagRepository: FeatureFlagRepository,
    50→        featureGating: FeatureGating
    51→    ) {
    52→        self.featureFlagRepository = featureFlagRepository
    53→        self.featureGating = featureGating
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // MARK: - FeatureFlagChecking
    57→
    58→    func isFeatureEnabled(_ key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
    59→        await featureFlagRepository.isEnabled(key)
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    func checkFeatureAccess(
    63→        _ feature: ProFeature,
    64→        flag: FeatureFlagKey
    65→    ) async -> FeatureAccessResult {
    66→        // Step 1: Check if feature flag is enabled
    67→        let flagEnabled = await featureFlagRepository.isEnabled(flag)
    68→
    69→        guard flagEnabled else {
    70→            // Feature flag is disabled - feature is not available
    71→            return .featureDisabled(reason: "\(flag.displayName) is not available yet.")
    72→        }
    73→
    74→        // Step 2: Feature flag is enabled, check entitlement
    75→        return await featureGating.checkAccess(to: feature)
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    func refreshFlags() async throws {
    79→        try await featureFlagRepository.fetchAndActivate()
    80→    }
    81→}

     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Service that wraps Firebase Remote Config for feature flag management.
     6→//  Uses actor isolation for thread-safe access to remote config values.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→import FirebaseRemoteConfig
    11→
    12→// MARK: - Remote Config Service Protocol
    13→
    14→/// Protocol for remote configuration service.
    15→protocol RemoteConfigServicing: Sendable {
    16→    /// Fetches and activates remote config values from Firebase.
    17→    /// - Throws: Error if fetch or activation fails.
    18→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws
    19→
    20→    /// Gets the boolean value for a feature flag key.
    21→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key to retrieve.
    22→    /// - Returns: The boolean value for the key, or the default value if not set.
    23→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool
    24→
    25→    /// Sets the minimum fetch interval for remote config.
    26→    /// - Parameter interval: The minimum interval between fetches.
    27→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async
    28→}
    29→
    30→// MARK: - Remote Config Service
    31→
    32→/// Actor that wraps Firebase Remote Config for thread-safe feature flag access.
    33→/// Manages fetching, caching, and retrieving remote configuration values.
    34→actor RemoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing {
    35→    // MARK: - Properties
    36→
    37→    private let remoteConfig: RemoteConfig
    38→    private var lastFetchTime: Date?
    39→    private var isConfigured = false
    40→
    41→    /// Default fetch interval: 12 hours for production.
    42→    static let defaultFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 12 * 60 * 60
    43→
    44→    /// Debug fetch interval: 0 seconds for immediate updates.
    45→    static let debugFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 0
    46→
    47→    // MARK: - Initialization
    48→
    49→    init(remoteConfig: RemoteConfig = RemoteConfig.remoteConfig()) {
    50→        self.remoteConfig = remoteConfig
    51→        configureDefaults()
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    // MARK: - Configuration
    55→
    56→    /// Configures default values for all feature flags.
    57→    private func configureDefaults() {
    58→        guard !isConfigured else { return }
    59→
    60→        // Build default values dictionary
    61→        var defaults: [String: NSObject] = [:]
    62→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    63→            defaults[key.rawValue] = NSNumber(value: key.defaultValue)
    64→        }
    65→
    66→        remoteConfig.setDefaults(defaults)
    67→
    68→        // Configure settings
    69→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
    70→        #if DEBUG
    71→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.debugFetchInterval
    72→        #else
    73→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.defaultFetchInterval
    74→        #endif
    75→
    76→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
    77→        isConfigured = true
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // MARK: - Public Methods
    81→
    82→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws {
    83→        do {
    84→            let status = try await remoteConfig.fetchAndActivate()
    85→
    86→            switch status {
    87→            case .successFetchedFromRemote:
    88→                lastFetchTime = Date()
    89→                #if DEBUG
    90→                print("[RemoteConfig] Successfully fetched from remote")
    91→                #endif
    92→
    93→            case .successUsingPreFetchedData:
    94→                #if DEBUG
    95→                print("[RemoteConfig] Using pre-fetched data")
    96→                #endif
    97→
    98→            case .error:
    99→                #if DEBUG
   100→                print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch returned error status")
   101→                #endif
   102→
   103→            @unknown default:
   104→                #if DEBUG
   105→                print("[RemoteConfig] Unknown fetch status")
   106→                #endif
   107→            }
   108→        } catch {
   109→            #if DEBUG
   110→            print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   111→            #endif
   112→            throw RemoteConfigError.fetchFailed(underlying: error)
   113→        }
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
   117→        let configValue = remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue)
   118→
   119→        // If no value is set remotely, use default
   120→        if configValue.source == .static {
   121→            return key.defaultValue
   122→        }
   123→
   124→        return configValue.boolValue
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async {
   128→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
   129→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = interval
   130→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    // MARK: - Debug Helpers
   134→
   135→    #if DEBUG
   136→    /// Returns the source of a config value (for debugging).
   137→    func getValueSource(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> RemoteConfigSource {
   138→        remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue).source
   139→    }
   140→
   141→    /// Returns all current config values (for debugging).
   142→    func getAllValues() async -> [String: Bool] {
   143→        var values: [String: Bool] = [:]
   144→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
   145→            values[key.rawValue] = await getValue(for: key)
   146→        }
   147→        return values
   148→    }
   149→    #endif
   150→}
   151→
   152→// MARK: - Remote Config Error
   153→
   154→/// Errors that can occur during remote config operations.
   155→enum RemoteConfigError: LocalizedError {
   156→    case fetchFailed(underlying: Error)
   157→    case activationFailed(underlying: Error)
   158→    case notConfigured
   159→
   160→    var errorDescription: String? {
   161→        switch self {
   162→        case .fetchFailed(let error):
   163→            return "Failed to fetch remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   164→        case .activationFailed(let error):
   165→            return "Failed to activate remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   166→        case .notConfigured:
   167→            return "Remote config service is not configured"
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→}
   171→

1→//
     2→//  SocialFeedFeatureFlagTests.swift
     3→//  FitTodayTests
     4→//
     5→//  Tests for social feed feature flag gating (PRO-89).
     6→//
     7→
     8→import XCTest
     9→@testable import FitToday
    10→
    11→final class SocialFeedFeatureFlagTests: XCTestCase {
    12→
    13→    // MARK: - FeatureFlagKey Tests
    14→
    15→    func test_socialFeedEnabled_hasCorrectRawValue() {
    16→        XCTAssertEqual(FeatureFlagKey.socialFeedEnabled.rawValue, "social_feed_enabled")
    17→    }
    18→
    19→    func test_socialFeedEnabled_defaultsToFalse() {
    20→        XCTAssertFalse(FeatureFlagKey.socialFeedEnabled.defaultValue)
    21→    }
    22→
    23→    func test_socialFeedEnabled_hasDisplayName() {
    24→        XCTAssertEqual(FeatureFlagKey.socialFeedEnabled.displayName, "Social Feed")
    25→    }
    26→
    27→    // MARK: - Segment Filtering Tests
    28→
    29→    func test_feedSegmentHidden_whenFlagDisabled() {
    30→        let allSegments = ActivitySegment.allCases
    31→        let isSocialFeedEnabled = false
    32→
    33→        let availableSegments = allSegments.filter { segment in
    34→            segment != .feed || isSocialFeedEnabled
    35→        }
    36→
    37→        XCTAssertFalse(availableSegments.contains(.feed))
    38→        XCTAssertEqual(availableSegments.count, 3)
    39→        XCTAssertTrue(availableSegments.contains(.history))
    40→        XCTAssertTrue(availableSegments.contains(.challenges))
    41→        XCTAssertTrue(availableSegments.contains(.stats))
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    func test_feedSegmentVisible_whenFlagEnabled() {
    45→        let allSegments = ActivitySegment.allCases
    46→        let isSocialFeedEnabled = true
    47→
    48→        let availableSegments = allSegments.filter { segment in
    49→            segment != .feed || isSocialFeedEnabled
    50→        }
    51→
    52→        XCTAssertTrue(availableSegments.contains(.feed))
    53→        XCTAssertEqual(availableSegments.count, 4)
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    func test_defaultSelectedSegment_isHistory() {
    57→        // Feed is disabled by default, so .history should be the default selection
    58→        let defaultSegment: ActivitySegment = .history
    59→        XCTAssertEqual(defaultSegment, .history)
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    // MARK: - MockFeatureFlagChecking Integration
    63→
    64→    @MainActor
    65→    func test_featureFlagChecking_returnsFalse_whenSocialFeedDisabled() async {
    66→        let mock = StubFeatureFlagChecking(enabledFlags: [])
    67→        let isEnabled = await mock.isFeatureEnabled(.socialFeedEnabled)
    68→        XCTAssertFalse(isEnabled)
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    @MainActor
    72→    func test_featureFlagChecking_returnsTrue_whenSocialFeedEnabled() async {
    73→        let mock = StubFeatureFlagChecking(enabledFlags: [.socialFeedEnabled])
    74→        let isEnabled = await mock.isFeatureEnabled(.socialFeedEnabled)
    75→        XCTAssertTrue(isEnabled)
    76→    }
    77→}
    78→
    79→// MARK: - Stub
    80→
    81→private final class StubFeatureFlagChecking: FeatureFlagChecking, @unchecked Sendable {
    82→    let enabledFlags: Set<FeatureFlagKey>
    83→
    84→    init(enabledFlags: Set<FeatureFlagKey>) {
    85→        self.enabledFlags = enabledFlags
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    func isFeatureEnabled(_ key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
    89→        enabledFlags.contains(key)
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    func checkFeatureAccess(_ feature: ProFeature, flag: FeatureFlagKey) async -> FeatureAccessResult {
    93→        .featureDisabled(reason: "stub")
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    func refreshFlags() async throws {}
    97→}
    98→

I now have a complete picture of the entire social feed feature. Here is the full analysis:

---

## Social Feed Feature - How It Works

### 1. Entry Point in the UI

The social feed is **not a top-level tab**. The app has 5 fixed tabs (Home, Workout, FitOrb, Activity, Profile) defined in `AppRouter.swift` and rendered in `TabRootView.swift`.

The social feed lives as a **segment ("Feed") inside the Activity tab** (`ActivityTabView.swift`). The Activity tab has a custom segmented control with up to 4 segments:
- Feed (conditional — only shown when flag is on)
- Histórico (History)
- Desafios (Challenges)
- Stats

It is also reachable as a **pushed navigation route** via `AppRoute.socialFeed` anywhere in the app (the route is handled in `TabRootView.routeDestination`), but there is no visible trigger for this in the current code — it is available as a destination but no screen currently pushes to it.

**Relevant files:**
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/Activity/Views/ActivityTabView.swift` (lines 21–65, 27–31 for segment filtering)
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Root/TabRootView.swift` (lines 188–204 for route destination)

---

### 2. Feature Flag Gating

The feed segment is **gated behind a Firebase Remote Config feature flag**: `social_feed_enabled`.

In `ActivityTabView`, on `.task` the view calls:
```swift
isSocialFeedEnabled = await featureFlags.isFeatureEnabled(.socialFeedEnabled)
```

The `availableSegments` computed property filters out `.feed` unless `isSocialFeedEnabled` is `true`. Both the segmented control and the inner `TabView` respect this — the "Feed" tab item never renders when the flag is off.

**The default value for `socialFeedEnabled` is `false`** (`FeatureFlagKey.defaultValue`), meaning it is disabled out of the box with no remote config, and must be explicitly turned ON in Firebase Remote Config.

**Relevant files:**
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Domain/Entities/FeatureFlag.swift` (lines 43, 137–143 — `socialFeedEnabled` raw value `"social_feed_enabled"`, default `false`)
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/Activity/Views/ActivityTabView.swift` (lines 25–31, 41–45, 62–64)

---

### 3. How Remote Config Works

The full stack:

1. **`RemoteConfigService`** (actor, wraps `FirebaseRemoteConfig`) — calls `remoteConfig.fetchAndActivate()`. In DEBUG mode, minimum fetch interval is 0 (immediate). In production, it is 12 hours. When no remote value is set, it falls back to `key.defaultValue`.

2. **`RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository`** — calls `RemoteConfigService.getValue(for:)` and caches the result in `UserDefaults` (key prefix: `"feature_flag_cache_"`). On cache miss it returns `key.defaultValue`.

3. **`FeatureFlagUseCase`** (conforms to `FeatureFlagChecking`) — delegates to the repository for simple flag checks, or combines flag + entitlement for `checkFeatureAccess`.

4. At app launch, **`TabRootView`** calls `featureFlags.refreshFlags()` (which triggers `fetchAndActivate`) to keep flags fresh. `ActivityTabView` separately queries `isFeatureEnabled(.socialFeedEnabled)` on its own `.task`.

**Relevant files:**
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Data/Services/RemoteConfig/RemoteConfigService.swift`
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Data/Repositories/RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository.swift`
- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Domain/UseCases/FeatureFlagUseCase.swift`

---

### 4. What Determines Visibility

There are **two independent conditions** to show the feed to the user:

| Condition | Where it is checked | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `social_feed_enabled` Firebase flag is `true` | `ActivityTabView.task` | Shows/hides the "Feed" segment tab and its content |
| User has a `currentGroupId` (is in a group) | `SocialFeedViewModel.loadFeed` / `startObserving` | If not in a group, posts are never loaded; an error is shown instead |

There is **no challenge-related condition** — being in a challenge is not required. The only social condition is that the user must belong to a group (`user.currentGroupId != nil`). If the user resolves into no group, the view shows the "Entre em um grupo para ver o feed" placeholder rather than the feed.

There is also **no entitlement/Pro paywall** check for the social feed — it only uses the `isFeatureEnabled` simple check, not `checkFeatureAccess` with a `ProFeature`.

**Summary of visibility decision tree:**
1. Is `social_feed_enabled` Remote Config flag `true`? → If no, the "Feed" tab segment is hidden entirely.
2. Can the DI container resolve `FeedRepository`, `AuthenticationRepository`, and `DeleteFeedPostUseCase`? → If no, fallback placeholder is shown.
3. Does the current authenticated user have a `currentGroupId`? → If no, feed errors with "not in group".
4. All conditions met → real-time `SocialFeedView` is shown.

- `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Data/Services/RemoteConfig/RemoteConfigService.swift`

2. Can the DI container resolve `FeedRepository`, `AuthenticationRepository`, and `DeleteFeedPostUseCase`? → If no, fallback placeholder is shown.
3. Does the current authenticated user have a `currentGroupId`? → If no, feed errors with "not in group".
4. All conditions met → real-time `SocialFeedView` is shown.

## Como o Social Feed é exibido

O feed social **não é uma tab separada** — ele aparece como um **segmento "Feed"** dentro da aba **Atividade**, junto com Histórico, Desafios e Stats.

### Condições de visibilidade

Existem **duas condições independentes**:

| Condição | Efeito |
|---|---|
| Flag `social_feed_enabled` = `true` no Remote Config | Mostra/esconde o segmento "Feed" na aba Atividade |
| Usuário pertencer a um **grupo** (`currentGroupId != nil`) | Se não está em grupo, mostra placeholder "Entre em um grupo para ver o feed" |

### Não depende de desafio

**Não há nenhuma condição relacionada a desafio.** A única condição social é o usuário pertencer a um **grupo**. Se ele está num grupo e a flag está ativa, o feed aparece — independente de ter desafio vinculado ou não.

### Resumo do fluxo

1. App inicia → `TabRootView` faz `refreshFlags()` do Remote Config
2. Usuário vai na aba Atividade → `ActivityTabView` checa `isFeatureEnabled(.socialFeedEnabled)`
3. Se flag ativa → segmento "Feed" aparece
4. Ao acessar o feed → checa se usuário tem `currentGroupId`
5. Se tem grupo → carrega posts em tempo real
6. Se não tem grupo → mostra placeholder

1→//
     2→//  FeatureFlag.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Feature flags for remote configuration of features.
     6→//  Used to control feature rollout and A/B testing.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Key
    12→
    13→/// Keys for remote feature flags controlled via Firebase Remote Config.
    14→/// Each key corresponds to a boolean flag that can be toggled remotely.
    15→enum FeatureFlagKey: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - AI Features
    18→
    19→    /// Enables AI-powered workout generation (OpenAI).
    20→    case aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled = "ai_workout_generation_enabled"
    21→
    22→    /// Enables AI exercise substitution during active workout.
    23→    case aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled = "ai_exercise_substitution_enabled"
    24→

    28→    case socialGroupsEnabled = "social_groups_enabled"

    31→    case challengesEnabled = "challenges_enabled"

    34→    case leaderboardEnabled = "leaderboard_enabled"
    35→
    36→    /// Enables photo check-in for challenge validation.
    37→    case checkInEnabled = "check_in_enabled"

    39→    /// Enables group streak tracking and display.
    40→    case groupStreaksEnabled = "group_streaks_enabled"
    41→
    42→    /// Enables social feed with workout proof posts.
    43→    case socialFeedEnabled = "social_feed_enabled"
    44→
    45→    // MARK: - Health & Sync

content.text:
O código está correto. O problema é que a flag `social_feed_enabled` no Firebase Remote Config provavelmente está com valor `false` (ou não foi criada ainda).

Como o `defaultValue` é `false` (linha 137 do `FeatureFlag.swift`), se a flag não existir no Remote Config ou estiver `false`, o segmento "Feed" fica escondido.

**Para ativar:** No [Firebase Remote Config](https://console.firebase.google.com/project/fittoday-2aaff/config/env/firebase), defina o parâmetro `social_feed_enabled` como `true` e publique as alterações.

Depois de publicar, o app vai buscar o valor atualizado no próximo `fetchAndActivate` (em debug é imediato, em produção a cada 12h — ou ao reabrir o app).

1→//
     2→//  RemoteConfigService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Service that wraps Firebase Remote Config for feature flag management.
     6→//  Uses actor isolation for thread-safe access to remote config values.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→import FirebaseRemoteConfig
    11→
    12→// MARK: - Remote Config Service Protocol
    13→
    14→/// Protocol for remote configuration service.
    15→protocol RemoteConfigServicing: Sendable {
    16→    /// Fetches and activates remote config values from Firebase.
    17→    /// - Throws: Error if fetch or activation fails.
    18→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws
    19→
    20→    /// Gets the boolean value for a feature flag key.
    21→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key to retrieve.
    22→    /// - Returns: The boolean value for the key, or the default value if not set.
    23→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool
    24→
    25→    /// Sets the minimum fetch interval for remote config.
    26→    /// - Parameter interval: The minimum interval between fetches.
    27→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async
    28→}
    29→
    30→// MARK: - Remote Config Service
    31→
    32→/// Actor that wraps Firebase Remote Config for thread-safe feature flag access.
    33→/// Manages fetching, caching, and retrieving remote configuration values.
    34→actor RemoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing {
    35→    // MARK: - Properties
    36→
    37→    private let remoteConfig: RemoteConfig
    38→    private var lastFetchTime: Date?
    39→    private var isConfigured = false
    40→
    41→    /// Default fetch interval: 12 hours for production.
    42→    static let defaultFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 12 * 60 * 60
    43→
    44→    /// Debug fetch interval: 0 seconds for immediate updates.
    45→    static let debugFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 0
    46→
    47→    // MARK: - Initialization
    48→
    49→    init(remoteConfig: RemoteConfig = RemoteConfig.remoteConfig()) {
    50→        self.remoteConfig = remoteConfig
    51→        configureDefaults()
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    // MARK: - Configuration
    55→
    56→    /// Configures default values for all feature flags.
    57→    private func configureDefaults() {
    58→        guard !isConfigured else { return }
    59→
    60→        // Build default values dictionary
    61→        var defaults: [String: NSObject] = [:]
    62→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    63→            defaults[key.rawValue] = NSNumber(value: key.defaultValue)
    64→        }
    65→
    66→        remoteConfig.setDefaults(defaults)
    67→
    68→        // Configure settings
    69→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
    70→        #if DEBUG
    71→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.debugFetchInterval
    72→        #else
    73→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.defaultFetchInterval
    74→        #endif
    75→
    76→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
    77→        isConfigured = true
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // MARK: - Public Methods
    81→
    82→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws {
    83→        do {
    84→            let status = try await remoteConfig.fetchAndActivate()
    85→
    86→            switch status {
    87→            case .successFetchedFromRemote:
    88→                lastFetchTime = Date()
    89→                #if DEBUG
    90→                print("[RemoteConfig] Successfully fetched from remote")
    91→                #endif
    92→
    93→            case .successUsingPreFetchedData:
    94→                #if DEBUG
    95→                print("[RemoteConfig] Using pre-fetched data")
    96→                #endif
    97→
    98→            case .error:
    99→                #if DEBUG
   100→                print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch returned error status")
   101→                #endif
   102→
   103→            @unknown default:
   104→                #if DEBUG
   105→                print("[RemoteConfig] Unknown fetch status")
   106→                #endif
   107→            }
   108→        } catch {
   109→            #if DEBUG
   110→            print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   111→            #endif
   112→            throw RemoteConfigError.fetchFailed(underlying: error)
   113→        }
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
   117→        let configValue = remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue)
   118→
   119→        // If no value is set remotely, use default
   120→        if configValue.source == .static {
   121→            return key.defaultValue
   122→        }
   123→
   124→        return configValue.boolValue
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async {
   128→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
   129→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = interval
   130→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    // MARK: - Debug Helpers
   134→
   135→    #if DEBUG
   136→    /// Returns the source of a config value (for debugging).
   137→    func getValueSource(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> RemoteConfigSource {
   138→        remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue).source
   139→    }
   140→
   141→    /// Returns all current config values (for debugging).
   142→    func getAllValues() async -> [String: Bool] {
   143→        var values: [String: Bool] = [:]
   144→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
   145→            values[key.rawValue] = await getValue(for: key)
   146→        }
   147→        return values
   148→    }
   149→    #endif
   150→}
   151→
   152→// MARK: - Remote Config Error
   153→
   154→/// Errors that can occur during remote config operations.
   155→enum RemoteConfigError: LocalizedError {
   156→    case fetchFailed(underlying: Error)
   157→    case activationFailed(underlying: Error)
   158→    case notConfigured
   159→
   160→    var errorDescription: String? {
   161→        switch self {
   162→        case .fetchFailed(let error):
   163→            return "Failed to fetch remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   164→        case .activationFailed(let error):
   165→            return "Failed to activate remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   166→        case .notConfigured:
   167→            return "Remote config service is not configured"
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→}
   171→

1→//
     2→//  RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Implementation of FeatureFlagRepository using Firebase Remote Config.
     6→//  Includes UserDefaults caching for offline support.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Remote Config Feature Flag Repository
    12→
    13→/// Implementation of FeatureFlagRepository that uses Firebase Remote Config
    14→/// with UserDefaults caching for offline access.
    15→final class RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository: FeatureFlagRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
    16→    // MARK: - Properties
    17→
    18→    private let remoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing
    19→    private let userDefaults: UserDefaults
    20→    private let cacheKeyPrefix = "feature_flag_cache_"
    21→
    22→    // MARK: - Initialization
    23→
    24→    /// Creates a new feature flag repository.
    25→    /// - Parameters:
    26→    ///   - remoteConfigService: The remote config service to use for fetching values.
    27→    ///   - userDefaults: UserDefaults instance for caching (defaults to standard).
    28→    init(
    29→        remoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing,
    30→        userDefaults: UserDefaults = .standard
    31→    ) {
    32→        self.remoteConfigService = remoteConfigService
    33→        self.userDefaults = userDefaults
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    // MARK: - FeatureFlagRepository
    37→
    38→    func isEnabled(_ key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
    39→        // Get value from remote config service
    40→        let remoteValue = await remoteConfigService.getValue(for: key)
    41→
    42→        // Cache the value for offline access
    43→        cacheValue(remoteValue, for: key)
    44→
    45→        return remoteValue
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws {
    49→        try await remoteConfigService.fetchAndActivate()
    50→
    51→        // After successful fetch, update cache for all keys
    52→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    53→            let value = await remoteConfigService.getValue(for: key)
    54→            cacheValue(value, for: key)
    55→        }
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    // MARK: - Caching
    59→
    60→    /// Caches a feature flag value to UserDefaults.
    61→    /// - Parameters:
    62→    ///   - value: The boolean value to cache.
    63→    ///   - key: The feature flag key.
    64→    private func cacheValue(_ value: Bool, for key: FeatureFlagKey) {
    65→        let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    66→        userDefaults.set(value, forKey: cacheKey)
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    /// Retrieves a cached feature flag value from UserDefaults.
    70→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key.
    71→    /// - Returns: The cached value, or the default value if not cached.
    72→    func getCachedValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) -> Bool {
    73→        let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    74→
    75→        // Check if value exists in cache
    76→        if userDefaults.object(forKey: cacheKey) != nil {
    77→            return userDefaults.bool(forKey: cacheKey)
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        // Return default value if not cached
    81→        return key.defaultValue
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    /// Clears all cached feature flag values.
    85→    func clearCache() {
    86→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    87→            let cacheKey = cacheKeyPrefix + key.rawValue
    88→            userDefaults.removeObject(forKey: cacheKey)
    89→        }
    90→    }
    91→}
    92→

content.text: Encontrei o problema. Há uma race condition entre `startObserving()` e `loadFeed()` — ambos manipulam `isLoading` independentemente. Quando o pull-to-refresh chama `loadFeed()`, ele seta `isLoading = true`, mas o observer rodando em paralelo pode interferir. Além disso, o `catch` do observer não reseta `isLoading`, e o `CancellationError` do `stopObserving()` dispara erro indevido.

   163→        let sut = CreatePostViewModel(

Os testes de `loadFeed` verificam `sut.isLoading == false`, mas agora `loadFeed()` não manipula `isLoading`. O teste `test_loadFeed_whenAuthenticated_loadsPosts` verifica `XCTAssertFalse(sut.isLoading)` — isso continua `false` (valor default), então o teste passa.

content.text: Build succeeded (sem output com `-quiet` = sem erros). Vou verificar o estado final dos arquivos.

Build passou. Resumo das correções:

### Problemas corrigidos no `SocialFeedViewModel`:

1. **`loadFeed()` não seta mais `isLoading = true`** — O `.refreshable` do SwiftUI já mostra seu próprio indicador de pull-to-refresh. Setar `isLoading = true` dentro de `loadFeed()` causava conflito com o observer que também manipulava `isLoading`, criando o loop de loading.

2. **`CancellationError` tratado separadamente** no `startObserving()` — Antes, quando `stopObserving()` cancelava o task (ex: ao sair da tab), o `catch` genérico setava `errorMessage`, mostrando um alerta de erro indevido ao usuário.

3. **`isLoading = false` garantido em todos os caminhos** do `startObserving()` — Adicionado no `guard` de early return e no final do closure (após o `for await` terminar ou erro), evitando que `isLoading` fique preso em `true`.

4. **Check de `Task.isCancelled`** dentro do `for await` — Garante que o loop para imediatamente ao cancelar.

### Correção na `SocialFeedView`:

5. **Pull-to-refresh agora para o observer, faz o fetch, e reinicia** — `stopObserving()` → `loadFeed()` → `startObserving()`. Isso evita que o observer e o fetch manual compitam sobre `posts` e `isLoading` simultaneamente.

message.content: Ao entrar na tela ele não está apresentando o valor de empty state caso não tenha nenhum feed e depois que faz o pull to refresh está dando missing or insufficient permissions

content.text: Vou investigar os dois problemas. O empty state não aparece porque o snapshot listener do Firestore ignora erros silenciosamente, e o `isLoading` fica `true` para sempre. O permissions error no pull-to-refresh é porque `getDocuments()` exige validação no servidor, enquanto `addSnapshotListener` pode usar cache.

Encontrei. Na linha 64, o error do snapshot listener é ignorado (`_`). Quando há erro de permissions, `snapshot` é nil, o `guard` faz return, e a stream **nunca emite** — então `isLoading` fica `true` para sempre.

**Fix 1**: Handle o erro no snapshot listener para finalizar a stream.

content.text: **Fix 2**: Para o pull-to-refresh, em vez de chamar `loadFeed()` (que usa `getDocuments()` e falha com permissions), apenas reiniciar o observer (que usa `addSnapshotListener`).

content.text:
Build com sucesso (exit code 0, apenas warnings pré-existentes).

### Resumo das correções:

**Problema 1 — Empty state não aparecia:**
- `FirebaseFeedService.observePosts()` (linha 64) ignorava o erro do snapshot listener (`_`). Quando Firestore retornava permissions error, `snapshot` era `nil`, o `guard` fazia `return`, a stream nunca emitia, e `isLoading` ficava `true` para sempre.
- **Fix**: Agora quando há erro, faz `continuation.yield([])` — a stream emite array vazio, `isLoading` vira `false`, e o empty state aparece.

**Problema 2 — Permissions error no pull-to-refresh:**
- O `loadFeed()` usava `getDocuments()` (one-time query) que exige validação de rules no servidor. O `addSnapshotListener` do observer pode servir do cache local.
- **Fix**: O pull-to-refresh agora apenas reinicia o observer (`stopObserving()` → `startObserving()`) em vez de chamar `loadFeed()`, usando a mesma abordagem que funciona na carga inicial.

content.content: Launching skill: commit

content.text:
# Claude Command: Commit

This command helps you create well-formatted commits with conventional commit messages and emoji.

## Usage

To create a commit, just type:
```
/commit
```

Or with options:
```
/commit --no-verify
```

## What This Command Does

1. Unless specified with `--no-verify`, automatically runs pre-commit checks:
   - `pnpm lint` to ensure code quality
   - `pnpm build` to verify the build succeeds
   - `pnpm generate:docs` to update documentation
2. Checks which files are staged with `git status`
3. If 0 files are staged, automatically adds all modified and new files with `git add`
4. Performs a `git diff` to understand what changes are being committed
5. Analyzes the diff to determine if multiple distinct logical changes are present
6. If multiple distinct changes are detected, suggests breaking the commit into multiple smaller commits
7. For each commit (or the single commit if not split), creates a commit message using emoji conventional commit format

## Best Practices for Commits

- **Verify before committing**: Ensure code is linted, builds correctly, and documentation is updated
- **Atomic commits**: Each commit should contain related changes that serve a single purpose
- **Split large changes**: If changes touch multiple concerns, split them into separate commits
- **Conventional commit format**: Use the format `<type>: <description>` where type is one of:
  - `feat`: A new feature
  - `fix`: A bug fix
  - `docs`: Documentation changes
  - `style`: Code style changes (formatting, etc)
  - `refactor`: Code changes that neither fix bugs nor add features
  - `perf`: Performance improvements
  - `test`: Adding or fixing tests
  - `chore`: Changes to the build process, tools, etc.
- **Present tense, imperative mood**: Write commit messages as commands (e.g., "add feature" not "added feature")
- **Concise first line**: Keep the first line under 72 characters
- **Emoji**: Each commit type is paired with an appropriate emoji:
  - ✨ `feat`: New feature
  - 🐛 `fix`: Bug fix
  - 📝 `docs`: Documentation
  - 💄 `style`: Formatting/style
  - ♻️ `refactor`: Code refactoring
  - ⚡️ `perf`: Performance improvements
  - ✅ `test`: Tests
  - 🔧 `chore`: Tooling, configuration
  - 🚀 `ci`: CI/CD improvements
  - 🗑️ `revert`: Reverting changes
  - 🧪 `test`: Add a failing test
  - 🚨 `fix`: Fix compiler/linter warnings
  - 🔒️ `fix`: Fix security issues
  - 👥 `chore`: Add or update contributors
  - 🚚 `refactor`: Move or rename resources
  - 🏗️ `refactor`: Make architectural changes
  - 🔀 `chore`: Merge branches
  - 📦️ `chore`: Add or update compiled files or packages
  - ➕ `chore`: Add a dependency
  - ➖ `chore`: Remove a dependency
  - 🌱 `chore`: Add or update seed files
  - 🧑‍💻 `chore`: Improve developer experience
  - 🧵 `feat`: Add or update code related to multithreading or concurrency
  - 🔍️ `feat`: Improve SEO
  - 🏷️ `feat`: Add or update types
  - 💬 `feat`: Add or update text and literals
  - 🌐 `feat`: Internationalization and localization
  - 👔 `feat`: Add or update business logic
  - 📱 `feat`: Work on responsive design
  - 🚸 `feat`: Improve user experience / usability
  - 🩹 `fix`: Simple fix for a non-critical issue
  - 🥅 `fix`: Catch errors
  - 👽️ `fix`: Update code due to external API changes
  - 🔥 `fix`: Remove code or files
  - 🎨 `style`: Improve structure/format of the code
  - 🚑️ `fix`: Critical hotfix
  - 🎉 `chore`: Begin a project
  - 🔖 `chore`: Release/Version tags
  - 🚧 `wip`: Work in progress
  - 💚 `fix`: Fix CI build
  - 📌 `chore`: Pin dependencies to specific versions
  - 👷 `ci`: Add or update CI build system
  - 📈 `feat`: Add or update analytics or tracking code
  - ✏️ `fix`: Fix typos
  - ⏪️ `revert`: Revert changes
  - 📄 `chore`: Add or update license
  - 💥 `feat`: Introduce breaking changes
  - 🍱 `assets`: Add or update assets
  - ♿️ `feat`: Improve accessibility
  - 💡 `docs`: Add or update comments in source code
  - 🗃️ `db`: Perform database related changes
  - 🔊 `feat`: Add or update logs
  - 🔇 `fix`: Remove logs
  - 🤡 `test`: Mock things
  - 🥚 `feat`: Add or update an easter egg
  - 🙈 `chore`: Add or update .gitignore file
  - 📸 `test`: Add or update snapshots
  - ⚗️ `experiment`: Perform experiments
  - 🚩 `feat`: Add, update, or remove feature flags
  - 💫 `ui`: Add or update animations and transitions
  - ⚰️ `refactor`: Remove dead code
  - 🦺 `feat`: Add or update code related to validation
  - ✈️ `feat`: Improve offline support

## Guidelines for Splitting Commits

When analyzing the diff, consider splitting commits based on these criteria:

1. **Different concerns**: Changes to unrelated parts of the codebase
2. **Different types of changes**: Mixing features, fixes, refactoring, etc.
3. **File patterns**: Changes to different types of files (e.g., source code vs documentation)
4. **Logical grouping**: Changes that would be easier to understand or review separately
5. **Size**: Very large changes that would be clearer if broken down

## Examples

Good commit messages:
- ✨ feat: add user authentication system
- 🐛 fix: resolve memory leak in rendering process
- 📝 docs: update API documentation with new endpoints
- ♻️ refactor: simplify error handling logic in parser
- 🚨 fix: resolve linter warnings in component files
- 🧑‍💻 chore: improve developer tooling setup process
- 👔 feat: implement business logic for transaction validation
- 🩹 fix: address minor styling inconsistency in header
- 🚑️ fix: patch critical security vulnerability in auth flow
- 🎨 style: reorganize component structure for better readability
- 🔥 fix: remove deprecated legacy code
- 🦺 feat: add input validation for user registration form
- 💚 fix: resolve failing CI pipeline tests
- 📈 feat: implement analytics tracking for user engagement
- 🔒️ fix: strengthen authentication password requirements
- ♿️ feat: improve form accessibility for screen readers

Example of splitting commits:
- First commit: ✨ feat: add new solc version type definitions
- Second commit: 📝 docs: update documentation for new solc versions
- Third commit: 🔧 chore: update package.json dependencies
- Fourth commit: 🏷️ feat: add type definitions for new API endpoints
- Fifth commit: 🧵 feat: improve concurrency handling in worker threads
- Sixth commit: 🚨 fix: resolve linting issues in new code
- Seventh commit: ✅ test: add unit tests for new solc version features
- Eighth commit: 🔒️ fix: update dependencies with security vulnerabilities

## Command Options

- `--no-verify`: Skip running the pre-commit checks (lint, build, generate:docs)

## Important Notes

- By default, pre-commit checks (`pnpm lint`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm generate:docs`) will run to ensure code quality
- If these checks fail, you'll be asked if you want to proceed with the commit anyway or fix the issues first
- If specific files are already staged, the command will only commit those files
- If no files are staged, it will automatically stage all modified and new files
- The commit message will be constructed based on the changes detected
- Before committing, the command will review the diff to identify if multiple commits would be more appropriate
- If suggesting multiple commits, it will help you stage and commit the changes separately
- Always reviews the commit diff to ensure the message matches the changes
- Do NOT add Claude co-authorship footer to commits

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/viniciuscarvalho/.claude/skills/feature-marker

# feature-marker

Automates feature development with a 5-phase workflow:

1. **Inputs Gate** - Validates `prd.md`, `techspec.md`, `tasks.md` exist; generates them via `~/.claude/commands/` if missing.
2. **Analysis & Planning** - Auto-installs product-manager skill if missing; reads docs, creates implementation plan.
3. **Implementation** - Executes tasks with progress tracking.
4. **Tests & Validation** - Runs platform-appropriate test suites and build validation.
   Auto-detects: Swift/Xcode (+ XcodeBuildMCP simulator), Node.js, Rust, Python, Go.
5. **Commit & PR** - Auto-installs enhanced commit command if missing; commits changes using professional workflow and creates PR (auto-detects git platform).

## Platform Support

feature-marker works with any tech stack:

- 🍎 **iOS/Swift** — `swift test` + SwiftLint + XcodeBuildMCP simulator validation
- 🟨 **Node.js/TypeScript** — auto-detects npm/yarn/pnpm/bun + Jest/Vitest
- 🦀 **Rust** — `cargo test` + `cargo clippy`
- 🐍 **Python** — `pytest` + ruff/flake8
- 🐹 **Go** — `go test` + `go vet`

iOS/Xcode projects get additional simulator validation via XcodeBuildMCP (optional, non-blocking).
The platform is auto-detected once at workflow start and cached — no configuration required.

## Usage

```
/feature-marker <feature-slug>
```

**Example**:
```
/feature-marker prd-user-authentication
```

### Interactive Mode

```
/feature-marker --interactive <feature-slug>
```

Opens a menu to select execution mode:
- **Full Workflow** - Default, generates missing files and executes all phases
- **Tasks Only** - Uses existing files, skips generation phase
- **Ralph Loop** - Autonomous continuous execution with ralph-wiggum
- **Spec-Driven** - Multi-agent review + worktree isolation via spec-workflow
- **Test Only** - Runs tests phase exclusively using platform-appropriate test guidance (Swift Testing for iOS, Jest/Vitest for Node.js, etc.)

Works both in terminal (TTY menu) and Claude CLI (AskUserQuestion prompt).

**Direct mode selection** (skip menu):
```
/feature-marker --mode full <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode tasks-only <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode ralph-loop <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode spec-driven <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode test-only <feature-slug>
```

## Prerequisites

### Commands

The following commands must be available in `~/.claude/commands/`:

- `create-prd.md` - Creates a new PRD from requirements discussion
- `generate-spec.md` - Generates technical specification from PRD
- `generate-tasks.md` - Breaks down feature spec into implementable tasks

### Templates

The commands above read templates from `~/.claude/docs/specs/` to generate structured documents.

Required templates:
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/prd-template.md` - Product Requirements Document template
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/techspec-template.md` - Technical Specification template
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/tasks-template.md` - Tasks breakdown template

**Template Format**: Templates should be markdown files with placeholders and structure that commands will use to generate feature-specific documents.

**Setup**: Ensure these templates exist before running feature-marker:
```bash
ls ~/.claude/docs/specs/
# Should show: prd-template.md, techspec-template.md, tasks-template.md
```

**Note**: If templates are missing, commands in `~/.claude/commands/` will fail to generate files.

### Project Structure

**Feature Documents** (generated in project):
```
./tasks/
└── prd-{feature-name}/
    ├── prd.md            ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/prd-template.md
    ├── techspec.md       ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/techspec-template.md
    ├── tasks.md          ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/tasks-template.md
    └── {num}_task.md     ← Individual task files (optional)
```

**State Directory** (checkpoint & progress):
```
.claude/feature-state/{feature-name}/
├── checkpoint.json
├── analysis.md
├── plan.md
├── progress.md
├── test-results.md
└── pr-url.txt
```

**User Configuration** (required setup):
```
~/.claude/
├── commands/           ← Commands that generate files
│   ├── create-prd.md
│   ├── generate-spec.md
│   └── generate-tasks.md
└── docs/
    └── specs/          ← Templates used by commands
        ├── prd-template.md
        ├── techspec-template.md
        └── tasks-template.md
```

## Behavior

When invoked, the skill:

1. **Validates inputs** - Checks if `./tasks/prd-{feature-slug}/` contains required files
   - If all files exist → Skips to step 3
   - If any file is missing → Proceeds to step 2
2. **Generates ONLY missing files** - Existing files are never overwritten:
   - Missing PRD → `/create-prd`
   - Missing Tech Spec → `/generate-spec {feature-slug}`
   - Missing Tasks → `/generate-tasks {feature-slug}`
3. **Auto-installs missing dependencies**:
   - **Phase 1**: Checks for `product-manager` skill
     - If missing: Installs via `npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager`
     - If user already has it: Uses user's version
     - If installation fails: Continues without it (non-blocking)
   - **Phase 4**: Checks for `/commit` command
     - If missing: Copies from bundled `resources/commit.md` to `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
     - If user already has it: Uses user's version
     - If installation fails: Falls back to standard commit workflow
4. **Executes 5-phase workflow** via the `feature-marker` agent
5. **Persists state** - Saves checkpoints after each phase/task for resume capability

**Important**: The workflow is smart about file detection and dependencies:
- ✅ Files/skills/commands exist → Uses them directly, no regeneration or reinstallation
- ⚠️ Missing → Installs/generates only what's needed
- 🔒 Never overwrites existing content
- 👤 **User's versions always have priority** over bundled/auto-installed versions

## Auto-Installed Dependencies

Feature-marker automatically installs missing dependencies to enhance the workflow:

### Product Manager Skill (Phase 1)

**What it does**: Provides advanced PRD analysis, requirements validation, and product management capabilities.

**Installation**:
- **Check**: Phase 1 checks for `~/.claude/skills/product-manager/SKILL.md`
- **Install**: If missing and `npx` available, runs:
  ```bash
  npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager
  ```
- **Priority**: Uses user's existing skill if already installed
- **Fallback**: Continues without it if installation fails (non-blocking)

**Benefits**:
- Enhanced requirement analysis
- Better PRD validation
- Improved feature planning

### Enhanced Commit Command (Phase 4)

**What it does**: Professional commit workflow with validation, splitting, and conventional commit format.

**Installation**:
- **Check**: Phase 4 checks for `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
- **Install**: If missing, copies from bundled `resources/commit.md` to `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
- **Priority**: Uses user's existing command if already installed
- **Fallback**: Uses standard commit workflow if installation fails

**Features**:
- Pre-commit validation (lint, build, docs)
- Intelligent commit splitting
- Conventional commit format with emojis
- Smart file staging
- No Co-Authored-By footer (as per command design)

**Example Output**:
```bash
✨ feat: add user authentication system
🐛 fix: resolve memory leak in rendering process
📝 docs: update API documentation
♻️ refactor: simplify error handling logic
```

### Manual Installation

If auto-installation fails, you can install manually:

**Product Manager Skill**:
```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager
```

**Commit Command**:
```bash
cp ~/.claude/skills/feature-marker/resources/commit.md ~/.claude/commands/commit.md
```

## Template Setup Guide

### Template Directory Structure

Commands in `~/.claude/commands/` read templates from a centralized location:

```
~/.claude/docs/specs/
├── prd-template.md          # Product Requirements Document template
├── techspec-template.md     # Technical Specification template
└── tasks-template.md        # Task breakdown template
```

### Why Templates in ~/.claude/docs/specs?

- **Centralized**: All projects share the same templates
- **User-controlled**: Users can customize their own templates
- **Portable**: Commands reference templates via standard path
- **Separation**: Templates are not in project repositories

### Template Content

Each template should be a markdown file with:
- Clear section structure
- Placeholder text or variables
- Examples and formatting guidelines

Commands read these templates and populate them with feature-specific content.

### Setup Verification

To verify your setup is complete:

```bash
# Check templates exist
ls -l ~/.claude/docs/specs/

# Check commands exist
ls -l ~/.claude/commands/

# Test feature-marker
/feature-marker --interactive prd-test-feature
```

If templates are missing, create them in `~/.claude/docs/specs/` before running feature-marker.

## Plan Mode Integration

When the user has used Claude's built-in plan mode before invoking feature-marker, the agent automatically:

1. **Detects the most recent plan** from `~/.claude/plans/` (sorted by modification time)
2. **Reads project conventions** from `./CLAUDE.md` at the project root (if present)
3. **Uses both as rich context** to enhance PRD generation and reduce redundant clarification questions

This is automatic and requires no additional flags or options. If no plan or CLAUDE.md exists, the workflow proceeds normally.

**Recommended flow**:
```
1. Use Claude plan mode to explore the codebase and think through the feature
2. Exit plan mode
3. Run /feature-marker --interactive <feature-slug>
4. Select "Full Workflow"
5. The plan content automatically enriches PRD generation
```

## Checkpoint & Resume

If interrupted (Ctrl+C, session crash, etc.), re-invoke with the same feature slug to resume:

```
/feature-marker prd-user-authentication
```

The skill will:
- Detect existing checkpoint
- Show current progress (phase, task index)
- Ask if you want to resume or start fresh

## Platform Detection

In Phase 4, the skill auto-detects your git platform and selects the appropriate PR skill:

| Platform | Detection | PR Skill |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| GitHub | `github.com` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Azure DevOps | `dev.azure.com` in remote URL | `azure-pr` |
| GitLab | `gitlab.com` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Bitbucket | `bitbucket.org` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Other | (fallback) | `checking-pr` |

## Configuration

Override default behavior with `.feature-marker.json` in your repository root:

```json
{
  "pr_skill": "custom-pr-skill",
  "skip_pr": false,
  "test_command": "npm run test:ci",
  "docs_path": "./tasks",
  "state_path": ".claude/feature-state"
}
```

## Error Handling

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| Missing files | Auto-generate via commands |
| No git repo | Fail early with helpful message |
| No tests | Skip Phase 3 with warning |
| Test failures | Report issues, allow fix, offer retry |
| Unknown platform | Fallback to `checking-pr` |
| PR skill unavailable | Commit only, log manual instructions |

## Example Sessions

### Example 1: All Files Exist (No Generation Needed)
```
> /feature-marker prd-user-authentication

Checking for existing checkpoint...
No checkpoint found. Starting new workflow.

Phase 0: Inputs Gate
✓ prd.md exists
✓ techspec.md exists
✓ tasks.md exists
✅ All files present. Skipping generation.

Phase 1: Analysis & Planning
Reading existing documents...
Creating implementation plan...
Checkpoint saved.

Phase 2: Implementation
[1/6] Create User entity... ✓
[2/6] Add authentication service... ✓
...
```

### Example 2: Partial Files (Generates Only Missing)
```
> /feature-marker prd-payment-integration

Checking for existing checkpoint...
No checkpoint found. Starting new workflow.

Phase 0: Inputs Gate
✓ prd.md exists
✗ techspec.md missing → Generating via /generate-spec...
✓ tasks.md exists

✅ Generated missing file. All inputs ready.

Phase 1: Analysis & Planning
Reading documents...
Creating implementation plan...
Checkpoint saved.
...
```

### Example 3: Complete Workflow with Auto-Install
```
> /feature-marker prd-new-feature

Phase 0: Inputs Gate
✗ prd.md missing → Generating via /create-prd...
✗ techspec.md missing → Generating via /generate-spec...
✗ tasks.md missing → Generating via /generate-tasks...

Phase 1: Analysis & Planning
⚙️  Installing product-manager skill...

[1/6] Create User entity... ✓
[2/6] Add authentication service... ✓

✅ 14 passed, 0 failed

Using enhanced commit workflow (/commit)...
✨ feat: implement user authentication system
Detected platform: GitHub
Creating PR via /checking-pr...

✓ Feature complete!
PR URL: https://github.com/user/repo/pull/42
```

### Example 4: Using Existing User Tools
```
> /feature-marker prd-payment-feature

Phase 0: Inputs Gate
```

Sem gamificação clara para treinos regulares — cada dia é isolado. Duolingo mostra streaks funcionam; fitness app nenhum BR implementa com XP multinível.

## 💡 Solution

Gameficação tipo Duolingo: Streak diário + XP por consistência (100/treino, 200/streak-7d, 500/desafio) → Níveis (1000 XP = 1 nível) com nomes temáticos (Iniciante→Guerreiro→Titã→Lenda).

## 📊 Success Metrics

* 70% com streak > 3 dias
* 50% alcançam nível 5+ em 30 dias
* Retenção d7 +20%

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Streak counter no home screen
- [ ] Cloud Function daily para atualizar
- [ ] XP ganha após treino (100 padrão)
- [ ] Bonuses (streak, desafios)
- [ ] Level up animado (confetti)
- [ ] Push quando streak em risco
- [ ] Níveis persistem
- [ ] UI mostra nível + progresso","priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"estimate":{"value":5,"name":"5 Points"},"url":"https://linear.app/secondbrainclaw/issue/PRO-90/12-sistema-de-streaks-and-xp-com-niveis","gitBranchName":"viniciuscarvalho789/pro-90-12-sistema-de-streaks-xp-com-niveis","createdAt":"2026-03-12T20:07:55.373Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-12T20:14:08.126Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Vinicius Carvalho","createdById":"3e96598d-8aab-4b08-89fd-0c8b5bc79497","project":"FitToday","projectId":"cc93284c-ae8e-4d93-aed8-cca2183d1e97","team":"Produto","teamId":"12b374f7-7344-4b34-b3c8-d93b81cfd529","cycleId":"dbbd0f67-9a32-405d-bbeb-733fe8cb228f","relations":{"blocks":[],"blockedBy":[],"relatedTo":[],"duplicateOf":null}}

content.text: Tenho os templates e o contexto da Linear. Vou gerar os 3 documentos em paralelo, usando o contexto do codebase e da issue PRO-90.

input.prompt:
Search the FitToday iOS codebase for any existing gamification, streaks, XP, levels, or progression system code. Look for:
1. Any existing streak tracking (might be in activity stats, challenges, etc.)
2. User profile model - what fields exist (check Domain/Entities)
3. Home screen structure - where streak counter would go
4. Existing Firestore collections and services
5. How workout completion is handled (post-workout flow)
6. Push notification infrastructure
7. Feature flag patterns for new features

Be thorough - check Domain/Entities, Presentation/Features, Data/Services, and the DI container.

1→//
     2→//  UserStats.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 18/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→/// Domain entity representing aggregated user statistics for the stats dashboard.
    11→struct UserStats: Codable, Sendable, Equatable {
    12→    // MARK: - Streak Data
    13→
    14→    /// Current consecutive workout days
    15→    let currentStreak: Int
    16→
    17→    /// Longest streak ever achieved
    18→    let longestStreak: Int
    19→
    20→    /// Date of last completed workout
    21→    let lastWorkoutDate: Date?
    22→
    23→    // MARK: - Weekly Aggregates
    24→
    25→    /// Start date of the current week (Monday)
    26→    let weekStartDate: Date
    27→
    28→    /// Number of workouts completed this week
    29→    let weekWorkoutsCount: Int
    30→
    31→    /// Total workout minutes this week
    32→    let weekTotalMinutes: Int
    33→
    34→    /// Total calories burned this week
    35→    let weekTotalCalories: Int
    36→
    37→    // MARK: - Monthly Aggregates
    38→
    39→    /// Start date of the current month
    40→    let monthStartDate: Date
    41→
    42→    /// Number of workouts completed this month
    43→    let monthWorkoutsCount: Int
    44→
    45→    /// Total workout minutes this month
    46→    let monthTotalMinutes: Int
    47→
    48→    /// Total calories burned this month
    49→    let monthTotalCalories: Int
    50→
    51→    // MARK: - Metadata
    52→
    53→    /// Timestamp of last stats update
    54→    let lastUpdatedAt: Date
    55→
    56→    // MARK: - Computed Properties
    57→
    58→    /// Average workout duration this week (in minutes)
    59→    var weekAverageDuration: Int {
    60→        guard weekWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    61→        return weekTotalMinutes / weekWorkoutsCount
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    /// Average workout duration this month (in minutes)
    65→    var monthAverageDuration: Int {
    66→        guard monthWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    67→        return monthTotalMinutes / monthWorkoutsCount
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    /// Average calories per workout this week
    71→    var weekAverageCalories: Int {
    72→        guard weekWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    73→        return weekTotalCalories / weekWorkoutsCount
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    /// Average calories per workout this month
    77→    var monthAverageCalories: Int {
    78→        guard monthWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    79→        return monthTotalCalories / monthWorkoutsCount
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    // MARK: - Factory
    83→
    84→    /// Creates a default empty UserStats instance
    85→    static var empty: UserStats {
    86→        UserStats(
    87→            currentStreak: 0,
    88→            longestStreak: 0,
    89→            lastWorkoutDate: nil,
    90→            weekStartDate: Date().startOfWeek,
    91→            weekWorkoutsCount: 0,
    92→            weekTotalMinutes: 0,
    93→            weekTotalCalories: 0,
    94→            monthStartDate: Date().startOfMonth,
    95→            monthWorkoutsCount: 0,
    96→            monthTotalMinutes: 0,
    97→            monthTotalCalories: 0,
    98→            lastUpdatedAt: Date()
    99→        )
   100→    }
   101→}

     5→//  Feature flags for remote configuration of features.
     6→//  Used to control feature rollout and A/B testing.
