# Product Requirements Document (PRD)

**Project Name:** Sistema de Streaks & XP com Níveis
**Document Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Author:** Vinicius Carvalho
**Status:** Draft
**Linear Issue:** PRO-90

---

## Executive Summary

**Problem Statement:**
Sem gamificação clara para treinos regulares — cada dia é isolado. Duolingo mostra que streaks funcionam; nenhum app de fitness BR implementa com XP multinível.

**Proposed Solution:**
Gamificação tipo Duolingo: Streak diário (já existe) + XP por consistência (100/treino, 200/streak-7d, 500/desafio) → Níveis (1000 XP = 1 nível) com nomes temáticos (Iniciante→Guerreiro→Titã→Lenda). Level-up animado com confetti.

**Business Value:**
Aumentar retenção d7 em 20% e engajamento diário via feedback loops de progressão.

**Success Metrics:**
- 70% dos usuários ativos com streak > 3 dias
- 50% alcançam nível 5+ em 30 dias
- Retenção d7 +20%

---

## Project Overview

### Background
O FitToday já possui streak tracking básico (contagem de dias consecutivos no home screen via `WeekStreakRow` e `UserStats`). Porém, a progressão é puramente visual sem recompensa tangível. Não há XP, níveis, ou celebrações por marcos.

### Current State
- Streak counter existe no home (`WeekStreakRow` + `DailyStatsCard`)
- `UserStats` persiste `currentStreak` e `longestStreak` via SwiftData
- `UserStatsCalculator` calcula streaks a partir do `WorkoutHistoryEntry`
- Não há XP, níveis, badges, ou animações de level-up
- Não há push notifications para streak em risco

### Desired State
- Sistema de XP com ganho automático após treinos
- Níveis temáticos com progressão visual
- Animação de level-up (confetti) ao subir de nível
- Streak em risco com notificação in-app
- XP bonificado por streaks e desafios

---

## Goals and Objectives

### Business Goals
1. Aumentar retenção d7 em 20% via feedback loops de progressão
2. Aumentar DAU/MAU ratio com incentivo diário (streak + XP)
3. Criar base para futuro sistema de recompensas/marketplace

### User Goals
1. Sentir progresso tangível a cada treino
2. Ter motivação para manter consistência diária
3. Visualizar evolução de longo prazo (nível)

---

## User Personas

### Primary Persona: Usuário Casual
**Demographics:** 25-35 anos, treina 3-4x/semana, usa o app há < 3 meses

**Goals:**
- Manter rotina de treinos
- Ver progresso visível rapidamente

**Pain Points:**
- Cada dia parece isolado, sem sensação de acúmulo
- Perde motivação após falhar 1 dia

### Secondary Persona: Usuário Engajado
**Demographics:** 20-30 anos, treina 5-6x/semana, usa desafios e grupos

**Goals:**
- Competir e mostrar nível para o grupo
- Alcançar níveis mais altos

**Pain Points:**
- Falta de diferenciação entre quem treina mais vs menos

---

## Functional Requirements

### FR-001: XP Award System [MUST]

**Description:**
Após completar um treino, o usuário recebe XP automaticamente.

**XP Table:**
| Ação | XP |
|------|-----|
| Treino completado | 100 |
| Streak 7 dias (bonus) | 200 |
| Streak 30 dias (bonus) | 500 |
| Desafio completado | 500 |

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- XP é creditado após `UpdateUserStatsUseCase` no fluxo de workout completion
- XP acumula no perfil do usuário (persistido em Firestore + SwiftData)
- Total de XP nunca decresce

---

### FR-002: Level System [MUST]

**Description:**
XP acumulado determina o nível do usuário. Cada 1000 XP = 1 nível.

**Níveis Temáticos:**
| Nível | Nome | XP Necessário |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 1-4 | Iniciante | 0-3999 |
| 5-9 | Guerreiro | 4000-8999 |
| 10-14 | Titã | 9000-13999 |
| 15-19 | Lenda | 14000-18999 |
| 20+ | Imortal | 19000+ |

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Nível é calculado a partir do total de XP (derivado, não armazenado separadamente)
- Nome temático é exibido no perfil e no home screen
- Níveis persistem entre sessões

---

### FR-003: Level-Up Animation [MUST]

**Description:**
Quando o usuário sobe de nível, uma animação de confetti é exibida com o novo nível e nome temático.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Animação é exibida no `WorkoutCompletionView` quando há level-up
- Inclui confetti, novo nível, e nome temático
- É dismissible com tap ou timeout de 5s
- Respeita `accessibilityReduceMotion`

---

### FR-004: XP & Level Display on Home [MUST]

**Description:**
O home screen exibe o nível atual, XP total, e progresso para o próximo nível.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Componente visual próximo ao `WeekStreakRow` existente
- Mostra: nível atual, nome temático, barra de progresso XP
- Barra de progresso mostra XP atual / XP necessário para o próximo nível
- Animação suave na barra ao ganhar XP

---

### FR-005: Streak Risk In-App Notification [SHOULD]

**Description:**
Quando o usuário está em risco de perder o streak (não treinou hoje e já são 18h+), uma notificação in-app é exibida.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Usa o sistema de notificação in-app existente (`NotificationRepository`)
- Exibida no home screen como banner
- Mostra "Seu streak de X dias está em risco!"
- Não persiste após o dia mudar

---

### FR-006: Feature Flag Gate [MUST]

**Description:**
Todo o sistema de XP/Níveis é controlado por feature flag no Firebase Remote Config.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Nova flag `gamification_enabled` com default `false`
- Quando desativada, nenhum componente de XP/nível é visível
- XP não é calculado ou persistido quando flag está off

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001: Performance [MUST]
XP award e level calculation devem completar em < 100ms. Não pode impactar o tempo de carregamento do home screen.

### NFR-002: Data Consistency [MUST]
XP total deve ser consistente entre Firestore (remote) e SwiftData (local). Em caso de conflito, Firestore é source of truth.

### NFR-003: Offline Support [SHOULD]
XP deve ser creditado localmente mesmo offline. Sync com Firestore quando conectar.

---

## Out of Scope

1. **Push notifications via APNs/FCM** — Infraestrutura não existe, alto custo para MVP
2. **Leaderboard de XP/Níveis** — Futuro, após validar engagement
3. **Rewards/Marketplace** — Fase 2
4. **Cloud Functions para cálculo server-side** — Mantemos client-side no MVP
5. **Badges/Achievements** — Futuro

---

## Release Planning

### Phase 1: MVP
- Domain entities (XP, Level)
- XP award no workout completion
- Level calculation
- Home screen display (nível + progresso)
- Level-up animation (confetti)
- Feature flag gate

### Phase 2: Enhancement
- Streak risk notification in-app
- XP bonus por desafios
- Nível visível no perfil público (grupos)

### Phase 3: Optimization
- Leaderboard de XP
- Badges/Achievements
- Push notifications para streak em risco

---

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| XP inflation (muito fácil subir de nível) | Medium | Medium | Ajustar tabela de XP via Remote Config |
| Overhead no workout completion | High | Low | Medir performance, manter < 100ms |
| Data inconsistency local/remote | High | Medium | Firestore como source of truth, merge strategy |

input.content:
# Technical Specification

**Project Name:** Sistema de Streaks & XP com Níveis (PRO-90)
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Author:** Vinicius Carvalho
**Status:** Draft

---

## Overview

### Problem Statement
O FitToday tem streak tracking básico mas sem progressão tangível (XP/Níveis). Usuários não sentem acúmulo de valor ao treinar consistentemente.

### Proposed Solution
Adicionar sistema de XP e Níveis ao fluxo existente de workout completion, usando a arquitetura MVVM + @Observable do projeto. XP persiste em SwiftData (local) e Firestore (remote).

### Goals
- XP creditado automaticamente após treino
- Níveis temáticos derivados do XP total
- Level-up animation no workout completion
- Display de nível/progresso no home screen
- Feature flag gating via Remote Config

---

## Scope

### In Scope
- Domain entities: `UserXP`, `XPLevel`, `XPTransaction`
- Use case: `AwardXPUseCase`
- Repository: `XPRepository` (protocol + SwiftData + Firestore impl)
- UI: `XPLevelCard` (home), `LevelUpCelebrationView` (completion)
- Feature flag: `gamification_enabled`

### Out of Scope
- Push notifications (APNs/FCM)
- Server-side XP calculation (Cloud Functions)
- Leaderboard de XP
- Badges/Achievements

---

## Technical Approach

### Architecture Overview
Segue a arquitetura existente do FitToday: MVVM com @Observable, injeção via Swinject, persistência dual (SwiftData local + Firestore remote).

### Key Technologies
- **SwiftData**: Persistência local do XP (`SDUserXP`)
- **Firestore**: Persistência remota em `users/{uid}/xp`
- **SwiftUI**: UI components com animações
- **Firebase Remote Config**: Feature flag

### Layer Mapping

```
Domain/
├── Entities/
│   ├── UserXP.swift          # XP total, level, transactions
│   └── XPLevel.swift         # Enum com níveis temáticos
├── Protocols/
│   └── XPRepository.swift    # Repository protocol
└── UseCases/
    └── AwardXPUseCase.swift  # Lógica de award + level-up detection

Data/
├── Models/
│   └── SDUserXP.swift        # SwiftData model
├── DTOs/
│   └── FBUserXP.swift        # Firestore DTO
└── Repositories/
    └── SwiftDataXPRepository.swift  # Implementation

Presentation/
└── Features/
    ├── Home/Components/
    │   └── XPLevelCard.swift  # Home display
    └── Workout/
        └── LevelUpCelebrationView.swift  # Confetti animation
```

---

## Data Model

### Entity: UserXP (Domain)

```swift
struct UserXP: Codable, Sendable {
    var totalXP: Int
    var lastAwardDate: Date?

    var level: Int { totalXP / 1000 + 1 }
    var currentLevelXP: Int { totalXP % 1000 }
    var xpToNextLevel: Int { 1000 - currentLevelXP }
    var levelProgress: Double { Double(currentLevelXP) / 1000.0 }
    var levelTitle: XPLevel { XPLevel(level: level) }

    static let empty = UserXP(totalXP: 0)
}
```

### Entity: XPLevel (Domain)

```swift
enum XPLevel: String, Sendable {
    case iniciante = "Iniciante"
    case guerreiro = "Guerreiro"
    case tita = "Titã"
    case lenda = "Lenda"
    case imortal = "Imortal"

    init(level: Int) {
        switch level {
        case 1...4: self = .iniciante
        case 5...9: self = .guerreiro
        case 10...14: self = .tita
        case 15...19: self = .lenda
        default: self = .imortal
        }
    }

    var icon: String {
        switch self {
        case .iniciante: return "star"
        case .guerreiro: return "shield.fill"
        case .tita: return "bolt.fill"
        case .lenda: return "crown.fill"
        case .imortal: return "flame.fill"
        }
    }
}
```

### Entity: XPTransaction (Domain)

```swift
enum XPTransactionType: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case workoutCompleted
    case streakBonus7
    case streakBonus30
    case challengeCompleted
}

struct XPTransaction: Codable, Sendable {
    let type: XPTransactionType
    let amount: Int
    let date: Date

    static func xpAmount(for type: XPTransactionType) -> Int {
        switch type {
        case .workoutCompleted: return 100
        case .streakBonus7: return 200
        case .streakBonus30: return 500
        case .challengeCompleted: return 500
        }
    }
}
```

### SwiftData Model: SDUserXP

```swift
@Model
final class SDUserXP {
    @Attribute(.unique) var id: String  // "current"
    var totalXP: Int
    var lastAwardDate: Date?

    init(totalXP: Int = 0) {
        self.id = "current"
        self.totalXP = totalXP
    }

    func toDomain() -> UserXP {
        UserXP(totalXP: totalXP, lastAwardDate: lastAwardDate)
    }
}
```

### Firestore DTO: FBUserXP

```swift
struct FBUserXP: Codable, Sendable {
    var totalXP: Int
    var lastAwardDate: Date?
    var level: Int
}
```

Stored at: `users/{uid}` (campos adicionais no documento existente)

---

## Repository

### Protocol: XPRepository

```swift
protocol XPRepository: Sendable {
    func getUserXP() async throws -> UserXP
    func awardXP(transaction: XPTransaction) async throws -> UserXP
    func syncFromRemote() async throws
}
```

### Implementation: SwiftDataXPRepository

- Lê/escreve `SDUserXP` localmente
- Sync com Firestore via `FirebaseUserService` (campos no doc do user)
- Singleton pattern (`id = "current"`) como `SDUserStats`

---

## Use Cases

### AwardXPUseCase

```swift
struct XPAwardResult: Sendable {
    let previousLevel: Int
    let newLevel: Int
    let xpAwarded: Int
    let totalXP: Int
    let didLevelUp: Bool
}

final class AwardXPUseCase {
    func execute(type: XPTransactionType, currentStreak: Int) async throws -> XPAwardResult
}
```

**Logic:**
1. Get current XP from repository
2. Calculate XP amount for transaction type
3. Check streak bonuses (7d → +200, 30d → +500)
4. Award XP via repository
5. Compare previous level vs new level
6. Return `XPAwardResult` with `didLevelUp` flag

---

## UI Components

### XPLevelCard (Home Screen)

Positioned after `WeekStreakRow` in `HomeView`. Shows:
- Level number + nome temático
- SF Symbol icon do nível
- Progress bar (XP atual / 1000)
- XP text: "750 / 1000 XP"

Gated by `gamification_enabled` feature flag.

### LevelUpCelebrationView (Workout Completion)

Overlay shown in `WorkoutCompletionView` when `XPAwardResult.didLevelUp == true`:
- Confetti animation (Canvas-based)
- New level number + nome temático
- "Parabéns!" text
- Auto-dismiss after 5s or on tap
- Respects `accessibilityReduceMotion`

---

## Integration Points

### Workout Completion Flow

In `WorkoutCompletionView.task`:
1. (existing) Save workout to history
2. (existing) `SyncWorkoutCompletionUseCase.execute()`
3. (existing) `UpdateUserStatsUseCase.execute()`
4. **(NEW)** `AwardXPUseCase.execute(type: .workoutCompleted, currentStreak:)`
5. **(NEW)** If `didLevelUp` → show `LevelUpCelebrationView`

### HomeViewModel

Add to `loadUserData()`:
1. **(NEW)** Check `gamification_enabled` flag
2. **(NEW)** Load `UserXP` from repository
3. **(NEW)** Expose `userXP: UserXP?` to view

---

## Feature Flag

Add to `FeatureFlagKey`:
```swift
case gamificationEnabled = "gamification_enabled"  // default: false
```

---

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- `XPLevelTests`: Verify level calculation from XP amounts
- `AwardXPUseCaseTests`: Test XP award, streak bonuses, level-up detection
- `UserXPTests`: Test computed properties (level, progress, title)

### Coverage Target: 80%+

Focus: `AwardXPUseCase`, `UserXP`, `XPLevel`

---

## Dependencies

### External
| Dependency | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| SwiftData | iOS 17+ | Local persistence |
| Firestore | Existing | Remote persistence |
| Firebase Remote Config | Existing | Feature flag |

### Internal
- `UpdateUserStatsUseCase` — provides `currentStreak` for bonus calc
- `WorkoutCompletionView` — integration point for XP award
- `HomeViewModel` — integration point for display
- `AppContainer` — DI registration

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] XP awarded after every workout completion
- [ ] Level calculated correctly from XP total
- [ ] Level-up animation plays when reaching new level
- [ ] Home screen shows level + progress bar
- [ ] Feature flag gates all gamification UI
- [ ] 80%+ unit test coverage on business logic
- [ ] Performance: < 100ms for XP award flow

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **Social Feed Bug Fixes (PRO-89)**: User reported three issues with the social feed feature: (a) feed tab not appearing despite Remote Config being `true`, (b) pull-to-refresh causing infinite loading loop (primary concern with emphasis on Swift concurrency rules), (c) empty state not showing when no feed posts exist, and (d) "missing or insufficient permissions" error on pull-to-refresh.
   - **Commit and Push**: User explicitly asked to commit and push the social feed fixes to the current branch `feat/pro-89-social-feed`.
   - **PRO-90 Feature Planning**: User invoked `/feature-marker --interactive -prd-sistema-breaks-niveis` for Linear issue PRO-90 "Sistema de Streaks & XP com Níveis". Selected "Full Workflow" mode to generate PRD, TechSpec, and Tasks, then execute all implementation phases.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Swift 6 Strict Concurrency**: `@MainActor`, `nonisolated(unsafe)`, `nonisolated deinit`, `Task` isolation inheritance
   - **MVVM + @Observable**: Project architecture pattern with Swinject DI
   - **SwiftUI `.refreshable` and `.task`**: Lifecycle modifiers and their interaction with async observer patterns
   - **AsyncStream**: Firestore snapshot listeners wrapped in `AsyncStream` with `continuation.yield()`/`continuation.finish()`
   - **Firebase Remote Config**: Feature flags with `FeatureFlagKey` enum, `defaultValue`, and `RemoteConfigService` actor
   - **Firestore `addSnapshotListener` vs `getDocuments()`**: Snapshot listeners can serve from cache; `getDocuments()` requires server validation of security rules
   - **SwiftData**: Local persistence with singleton pattern (`id = "current"`)
   - **Dual persistence**: SwiftData (local) + Firestore (remote) with Firestore as source of truth

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`FitToday/Presentation/Features/SocialFeed/ViewModels/SocialFeedViewModel.swift`** — Core file with concurrency bug fixes
     - Removed `isLoading = true` / `defer { isLoading = false }` from `loadFeed()` to prevent race condition with observer
     - Added `CancellationError` handling in `startObserving()` catch block
     - Added `self.isLoading = false` in all exit paths (guard early return, after for-await loop, after catch)
     - Added `if Task.isCancelled { break }` inside the `for await` loop
     - Changed `stopObserving()` from `nonisolated` to normal `@MainActor` method
     - Changed `deinit` to `nonisolated deinit` with direct `observeTask?.cancel()`
     - Kept `nonisolated(unsafe)` on `observeTask` property (required for deinit access)
     ```swift
     func loadFeed() async {
         do {
             guard let user = try await authRepository.currentUser() else {
                 errorMessage = FeedError.notAuthenticated.localizedDescription
                 return
             }
             currentUserId = user.id
             currentGroupId = user.currentGroupId
             guard let groupId = user.currentGroupId else {
                 errorMessage = FeedError.notInGroup.localizedDescription
                 return
             }
             posts = try await feedRepository.getPosts(groupId: groupId, limit: 50, after: nil)
         } catch {
             errorMessage = error.localizedDescription
         }
     }

     func startObserving() {
         observeTask?.cancel()
         observeTask = Task { [weak self] in
             guard let self else { return }
             do {
                 guard let user = try await authRepository.currentUser(),
                       let groupId = user.currentGroupId else {
                     self.isLoading = false
                     return
                 }
                 currentUserId = user.id
                 currentGroupId = groupId
                 for await newPosts in feedRepository.observePosts(groupId: groupId) {
                     if Task.isCancelled { break }
                     self.posts = newPosts
                     self.isLoading = false
                 }
             } catch is CancellationError {
                 // Expected when stopObserving() is called
             } catch {
                 self.errorMessage = error.localizedDescription
             }
             self.isLoading = false
         }
         isLoading = true
     }
     ```

   - **`FitToday/Presentation/Features/SocialFeed/Views/SocialFeedView.swift`** — View with refreshable fix
     - Changed `.refreshable` from `await viewModel.loadFeed()` to `stopObserving()` + `startObserving()` to avoid `getDocuments()` permissions error
     ```swift
     .refreshable {
         viewModel.stopObserving()
         viewModel.startObserving()
     }
     .task {
         viewModel.startObserving()
     }
     .onDisappear {
         viewModel.stopObserving()
     }
     ```

   - **`FitToday/Data/Services/Firebase/FirebaseFeedService.swift`** — Snapshot listener error handling fix
     - Changed from ignoring errors (`snapshot, _`) to handling them (`snapshot, error`)
     - Yields empty array on error instead of silently returning (which caused stream to never emit, keeping `isLoading = true` forever)
     ```swift
     .addSnapshotListener { snapshot, error in
         if error != nil {
             continuation.yield([])
             return
         }
         guard let snapshot else { return }
         let posts = snapshot.documents.compactMap {
             try? $0.data(as: FBFeedPost.self)
         }
         continuation.yield(posts)
     }
     ```

   - **`FitToday/Domain/Entities/FeatureFlag.swift`** — Read for understanding feature flag pattern (not modified in this session)

   - **`FitToday/Data/Services/RemoteConfig/RemoteConfigService.swift`** — Read for understanding Remote Config flow (not modified)

   - **`FitToday/Data/Repositories/RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository.swift`** — Read for understanding caching (not modified)

   - **`FitToday/Data/Repositories/FirebaseFeedRepository.swift`** — Read for understanding the AsyncStream wrapping pattern (not modified)

   - **`FitToday/Presentation/Features/Activity/Views/ActivityTabView.swift`** — Read to understand social feed tab visibility logic (gated by `isSocialFeedEnabled` state)

   - **PRO-90 Generated Files:**
     - **`tasks/prd-sistema-breaks-niveis/prd.md`** — PRD with problem statement, solution (Duolingo-style XP/Levels), 6 functional requirements (XP Award, Level System, Level-Up Animation, Home Display, Streak Risk Notification, Feature Flag), NFRs, release phases, risks
     - **`tasks/prd-sistema-breaks-niveis/techspec.md`** — Technical specification with architecture (MVVM + @Observable + Swinject), data models (UserXP, XPLevel, XPTransaction, SDUserXP, FBUserXP), repository pattern, AwardXPUseCase, UI components (XPLevelCard, LevelUpCelebrationView), integration points (WorkoutCompletionView, HomeViewModel)
     - **`tasks/prd-sistema-breaks-niveis/tasks.md`** — 7 tasks: (1) Domain Entities + Feature Flag [S], (2) XP Repository [M], (3) AwardXP Use Case [M], (4) Home Screen XP Level Card [M], (5) Level-Up Celebration View [M], (6) Workout Completion Integration [S], (7) Unit Tests [M]

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Build error: `main actor-isolated property 'observeTask' can not be referenced from a nonisolated context`**: When `stopObserving()` was changed from `nonisolated` to `@MainActor`, the `deinit` (which is inherently nonisolated) couldn't access `observeTask`. Fixed by: (a) keeping `nonisolated(unsafe)` on `observeTask`, (b) using `nonisolated deinit` with direct `observeTask?.cancel()` instead of calling `stopObserving()`.
   - **Safety hook blocking git commit**: The `block-dangerous-commands.sh` hook falsely detected "curl .* | sh" pattern in heredoc commit messages. Fixed by using the `/commit` skill instead which uses a simpler `-m` flag format.
   - **xcodebuild simulator not found**: `iPhone 16` simulator didn't exist. Fixed by using `iPhone 17 Pro` from the available destinations list.
   - **Empty state not showing**: Root cause was `FirebaseFeedService.observePosts()` silently ignoring snapshot listener errors (line 64: `snapshot, _`). When Firestore returned a permissions error, `snapshot` was `nil`, the `guard` returned, the stream never emitted, and `isLoading` stayed `true` forever. Fixed by yielding empty array on error.
   - **Permissions error on pull-to-refresh**: `loadFeed()` used `getDocuments()` which requires server-side rule validation and failed. `addSnapshotListener` used by the observer can serve from cache. Fixed by making refreshable restart the observer instead of calling `loadFeed()`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Diagnosed the race condition between `loadFeed()` and `startObserving()` both writing to `isLoading` and `posts` concurrently on `@MainActor`

1→//
     2→//  FeatureFlag.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Feature flags for remote configuration of features.
     6→//  Used to control feature rollout and A/B testing.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Key
    12→
    13→/// Keys for remote feature flags controlled via Firebase Remote Config.
    14→/// Each key corresponds to a boolean flag that can be toggled remotely.
    15→enum FeatureFlagKey: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - AI Features
    18→
    19→    /// Enables AI-powered workout generation (OpenAI).
    20→    case aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled = "ai_workout_generation_enabled"
    21→
    22→    /// Enables AI exercise substitution during active workout.
    23→    case aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled = "ai_exercise_substitution_enabled"
    24→
    25→    // MARK: - Social Features
    26→
    27→    /// Enables group creation, joining, and management.
    28→    case socialGroupsEnabled = "social_groups_enabled"
    29→
    30→    /// Enables workout challenges within groups.
    31→    case challengesEnabled = "challenges_enabled"
    32→
    33→    /// Enables leaderboard ranking in groups.
    34→    case leaderboardEnabled = "leaderboard_enabled"
    35→
    36→    /// Enables photo check-in for challenge validation.
    37→    case checkInEnabled = "check_in_enabled"
    38→
    39→    /// Enables group streak tracking and display.
    40→    case groupStreaksEnabled = "group_streaks_enabled"
    41→
    42→    /// Enables social feed with workout proof posts.
    43→    case socialFeedEnabled = "social_feed_enabled"
    44→
    45→    // MARK: - Health & Sync
    46→
    47→    /// Enables Apple Health read/write integration.
    48→    case healthKitSyncEnabled = "healthkit_sync_enabled"
    49→
    50→    /// Enables activity stats with Swift Charts.
    51→    case statsChartsEnabled = "stats_charts_enabled"
    52→
    53→    // MARK: - Content
    54→
    55→    /// Enables structured training programs.
    56→    case programsEnabled = "programs_enabled"
    57→
    58→    /// Enables user-created workout templates.
    59→    case customWorkoutsEnabled = "custom_workouts_enabled"
    60→
    61→    /// Enables exercise library/explorer.
    62→    case exerciseLibraryEnabled = "exercise_library_enabled"
    63→
    64→    // MARK: - Personal Trainer
    65→
    66→    /// Enables personal trainer integration features.
    67→    /// When enabled, users can connect with personal trainers via CMS.
    68→    case personalTrainerEnabled = "personal_trainer_enabled"
    69→
    70→    /// Enables synchronization of workouts from CMS.
    71→    /// When enabled, trainer-assigned workouts sync to the user's app.
    72→    case cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = "cms_workout_sync_enabled"
    73→
    74→    /// Enables chat functionality with personal trainers.
    75→    /// When enabled, users can message their assigned trainer.
    76→    case trainerChatEnabled = "trainer_chat_enabled"
    77→
    78→    // MARK: - Monetization & UX
    79→
    80→    /// Enables paywall/Pro upgrade prompts.
    81→    case paywallEnabled = "paywall_enabled"
    82→
    83→    /// Enables App Store review request after workout completion.
    84→    case reviewRequestEnabled = "review_request_enabled"
    85→
    86→    // MARK: - Operational
    87→
    88→    /// Shows maintenance banner and disables write operations.
    89→    case maintenanceModeEnabled = "maintenance_mode_enabled"
    90→
    91→    /// Shows non-dismissable update dialog directing to App Store.
    92→    case forceUpdateEnabled = "force_update_enabled"
    93→
    94→    // MARK: - Display Name
    95→
    96→    /// Human-readable display name for the feature flag.
    97→    var displayName: String {
    98→        switch self {
    99→        // AI
   100→        case .aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled: return "AI Workout Generation"
   101→        case .aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled: return "AI Exercise Substitution"
   102→        // Social
   103→        case .socialGroupsEnabled: return "Social Groups"
   104→        case .challengesEnabled: return "Challenges"
   105→        case .leaderboardEnabled: return "Leaderboard"
   106→        case .checkInEnabled: return "Check-In"
   107→        case .groupStreaksEnabled: return "Group Streaks"
   108→        case .socialFeedEnabled: return "Social Feed"
   109→        // Health
   110→        case .healthKitSyncEnabled: return "Apple Health Sync"
   111→        case .statsChartsEnabled: return "Stats Charts"
   112→        // Content
   113→        case .programsEnabled: return "Training Programs"
   114→        case .customWorkoutsEnabled: return "Custom Workouts"
   115→        case .exerciseLibraryEnabled: return "Exercise Library"
   116→        // Personal Trainer
   117→        case .personalTrainerEnabled: return "Personal Trainer Integration"
   118→        case .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled: return "CMS Workout Sync"
   119→        case .trainerChatEnabled: return "Trainer Chat"
   120→        // Monetization
   121→        case .paywallEnabled: return "Paywall"
   122→        case .reviewRequestEnabled: return "Review Request"
   123→        // Operational
   124→        case .maintenanceModeEnabled: return "Maintenance Mode"
   125→        case .forceUpdateEnabled: return "Force Update"
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    // MARK: - Default Value
   130→
   131→    /// Default value when remote config is unavailable.
   132→    /// Existing features default to `true` (no regression).
   133→    /// Unreleased features default to `false`.
   134→    var defaultValue: Bool {
   135→        switch self {
   136→        // Unreleased features — disabled by default
   137→        case .socialFeedEnabled,
   138→             .personalTrainerEnabled,
   139→             .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled,
   140→             .trainerChatEnabled,
   141→             .maintenanceModeEnabled,
   142→             .forceUpdateEnabled:
   143→            return false
   144→
   145→        // All existing features — enabled by default
   146→        default:
   147→            return true
   148→        }
   149→    }
   150→}
   151→

1→//
     2→//  UserStats.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 18/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→/// Domain entity representing aggregated user statistics for the stats dashboard.
    11→struct UserStats: Codable, Sendable, Equatable {
    12→    // MARK: - Streak Data
    13→
    14→    /// Current consecutive workout days
    15→    let currentStreak: Int
    16→
    17→    /// Longest streak ever achieved
    18→    let longestStreak: Int
    19→
    20→    /// Date of last completed workout
    21→    let lastWorkoutDate: Date?
    22→
    23→    // MARK: - Weekly Aggregates
    24→
    25→    /// Start date of the current week (Monday)
    26→    let weekStartDate: Date
    27→
    28→    /// Number of workouts completed this week
    29→    let weekWorkoutsCount: Int
    30→
    31→    /// Total workout minutes this week
    32→    let weekTotalMinutes: Int
    33→
    34→    /// Total calories burned this week
    35→    let weekTotalCalories: Int
    36→
    37→    // MARK: - Monthly Aggregates
    38→
    39→    /// Start date of the current month
    40→    let monthStartDate: Date
    41→
    42→    /// Number of workouts completed this month
    43→    let monthWorkoutsCount: Int
    44→
    45→    /// Total workout minutes this month
    46→    let monthTotalMinutes: Int
    47→
    48→    /// Total calories burned this month
    49→    let monthTotalCalories: Int
    50→
    51→    // MARK: - Metadata
    52→
    53→    /// Timestamp of last stats update
    54→    let lastUpdatedAt: Date
    55→
    56→    // MARK: - Computed Properties
    57→
    58→    /// Average workout duration this week (in minutes)
    59→    var weekAverageDuration: Int {
    60→        guard weekWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    61→        return weekTotalMinutes / weekWorkoutsCount
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    /// Average workout duration this month (in minutes)
    65→    var monthAverageDuration: Int {
    66→        guard monthWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    67→        return monthTotalMinutes / monthWorkoutsCount
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    /// Average calories per workout this week
    71→    var weekAverageCalories: Int {
    72→        guard weekWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    73→        return weekTotalCalories / weekWorkoutsCount
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    /// Average calories per workout this month
    77→    var monthAverageCalories: Int {
    78→        guard monthWorkoutsCount > 0 else { return 0 }
    79→        return monthTotalCalories / monthWorkoutsCount
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    // MARK: - Factory
    83→
    84→    /// Creates a default empty UserStats instance
    85→    static var empty: UserStats {
    86→        UserStats(
    87→            currentStreak: 0,
    88→            longestStreak: 0,
    89→            lastWorkoutDate: nil,
    90→            weekStartDate: Date().startOfWeek,
    91→            weekWorkoutsCount: 0,
    92→            weekTotalMinutes: 0,
    93→            weekTotalCalories: 0,
    94→            monthStartDate: Date().startOfMonth,
    95→            monthWorkoutsCount: 0,
    96→            monthTotalMinutes: 0,
    97→            monthTotalCalories: 0,
    98→            lastUpdatedAt: Date()
    99→        )
   100→    }
   101→}
   102→

1→//
     2→//  SDUserStats.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 18/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→import SwiftData
    10→
    11→/// SwiftData model for aggregated user statistics.
    12→/// Uses singleton pattern with id = "current" to ensure only one stats record.
    13→@Model
    14→final class SDUserStats {
    15→    /// Singleton identifier - always "current"
    16→    @Attribute(.unique) var id: String
    17→
    18→    // MARK: - Streak Data
    19→
    20→    /// Current consecutive workout days
    21→    var currentStreak: Int
    22→
    23→    /// Longest streak ever achieved
    24→    var longestStreak: Int
    25→
    26→    /// Date of last completed workout (for streak calculation)
    27→    var lastWorkoutDate: Date?
    28→
    29→    // MARK: - Weekly Aggregates
    30→
    31→    /// Start date of the current week (Monday)
    32→    var weekStartDate: Date
    33→
    34→    /// Number of workouts completed this week
    35→    var weekWorkoutsCount: Int
    36→
    37→    /// Total workout minutes this week
    38→    var weekTotalMinutes: Int
    39→
    40→    /// Total calories burned this week
    41→    var weekTotalCalories: Int
    42→
    43→    // MARK: - Monthly Aggregates
    44→
    45→    /// Start date of the current month
    46→    var monthStartDate: Date
    47→
    48→    /// Number of workouts completed this month
    49→    var monthWorkoutsCount: Int
    50→
    51→    /// Total workout minutes this month
    52→    var monthTotalMinutes: Int
    53→
    54→    /// Total calories burned this month
    55→    var monthTotalCalories: Int
    56→
    57→    // MARK: - Metadata
    58→
    59→    /// Timestamp of last stats update (for cache validation)
    60→    var lastUpdatedAt: Date
    61→
    62→    // MARK: - Initialization
    63→
    64→    init(
    65→        id: String = "current",
    66→        currentStreak: Int = 0,
    67→        longestStreak: Int = 0,
    68→        lastWorkoutDate: Date? = nil,
    69→        weekStartDate: Date = Date().startOfWeek,
    70→        weekWorkoutsCount: Int = 0,
    71→        weekTotalMinutes: Int = 0,
    72→        weekTotalCalories: Int = 0,
    73→        monthStartDate: Date = Date().startOfMonth,
    74→        monthWorkoutsCount: Int = 0,
    75→        monthTotalMinutes: Int = 0,
    76→        monthTotalCalories: Int = 0,
    77→        lastUpdatedAt: Date = Date()
    78→    ) {
    79→        self.id = id
    80→        self.currentStreak = currentStreak
    81→        self.longestStreak = longestStreak
    82→        self.lastWorkoutDate = lastWorkoutDate
    83→        self.weekStartDate = weekStartDate
    84→        self.weekWorkoutsCount = weekWorkoutsCount
    85→        self.weekTotalMinutes = weekTotalMinutes
    86→        self.weekTotalCalories = weekTotalCalories
    87→        self.monthStartDate = monthStartDate
    88→        self.monthWorkoutsCount = monthWorkoutsCount
    89→        self.monthTotalMinutes = monthTotalMinutes
    90→        self.monthTotalCalories = monthTotalCalories
    91→        self.lastUpdatedAt = lastUpdatedAt
    92→    }
    93→}

     8→import Foundation
     9→import SwiftData
    10→import Swinject
    11→
    12→/// Responsável por montar e expor o container de dependências da aplicação.
    13→struct AppContainer {
    14→    let container: Container
    15→    let router: AppRouter
    16→    let modelContainer: ModelContainer
    17→
    18→    @MainActor
    19→    static func build() -> AppContainer {
    20→        let modelContainer = makeModelContainer()
    21→        return build(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    @MainActor
    25→    static func build(modelContainer: ModelContainer) -> AppContainer {
    26→        let container = Container()
    27→        container.register(ModelContainer.self) { _ in modelContainer }
    28→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    29→
    30→        let router = AppRouter()
    31→        container.register(AppRouting.self) { _ in router }
    32→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    33→
    34→        // Repositórios
    35→        container.register(UserProfileRepository.self) { _ in
    36→            SwiftDataUserProfileRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    37→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    38→
    39→        container.register(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self) { _ in
    40→            SwiftDataWorkoutHistoryRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    41→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    42→
    43→        container.register(UserStatsRepository.self) { _ in
    44→            SwiftDataUserStatsRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    45→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    46→        
    47→        // Workout Composition Cache (F7)
    48→        container.register(WorkoutCompositionCacheRepository.self) { _ in
    49→            SwiftDataWorkoutCompositionCacheRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    50→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    51→
    52→        // WorkoutBlocksRepository é registrado após configurar exercícios (para permitir enriquecimento de mídia/instruções).
    53→
    54→        // StoreKit Service e EntitlementRepository
    55→        // Nota: StoreKitService é @MainActor, então precisa ser criado no main thread
    56→        let storeKitService = StoreKitService()
    57→        container.register(StoreKitService.self) { _ in storeKitService }
    58→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    59→        
    60→        let entitlementRepository = StoreKitEntitlementRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer, storeKitService: storeKitService)
    61→        container.register(EntitlementRepository.self) { _ in entitlementRepository }
    62→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    63→        
    64→        // Feature Gating Use Case
    65→        let aiUsageTracker = SimpleAIUsageTracker()
    66→        container.register(AIUsageTracking.self) { _ in aiUsageTracker }
    67→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    68→        
    69→        container.register(FeatureGating.self) { resolver in
    70→            FeatureGatingUseCase(
    71→                entitlementRepository: resolver.resolve(EntitlementRepository.self)!,
    72→                usageTracker: resolver.resolve(AIUsageTracking.self)
    73→            )
    74→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    75→
    76→        // ========== REMOTE CONFIG & FEATURE FLAGS ==========
    77→
    78→        // Remote Config Service - wraps Firebase Remote Config
    79→        let remoteConfigService = RemoteConfigService()
    80→        container.register(RemoteConfigServicing.self) { _ in remoteConfigService }
    81→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    82→
    83→        // Feature Flag Repository - provides feature flag access with caching
    84→        container.register(FeatureFlagRepository.self) { resolver in
    85→            RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository(
    86→                remoteConfigService: resolver.resolve(RemoteConfigServicing.self)!
    87→            )
    88→        }
    89→        .inObjectScope(.container)
    90→
    91→        // Feature Flag Use Case - combines feature flags with entitlement
    92→        container.register(FeatureFlagChecking.self) { resolver in
    93→            FeatureFlagUseCase(
    94→                featureFlagRepository: resolver.resolve(FeatureFlagRepository.self)!,
    95→                featureGating: resolver.resolve(FeatureGating.self)!
    96→            )
    97→        }
    98→        .inObjectScope(.container)
    99→
   100→        // ========== END REMOTE CONFIG & FEATURE FLAGS ==========
   101→
   102→        // Firestore Exercise Service
   103→        let exerciseService = FirestoreExerciseService()
   104→        container.register(ExerciseServiceProtocol.self) { _ in exerciseService }
   105→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   106→
   107→        // Workout Blocks Repository
   108→        let blocksRepository = BundleWorkoutBlocksRepository()
   109→        container.register(WorkoutBlocksRepository.self) { _ in blocksRepository }
   110→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   111→
   112→        // Library Workouts Repository - using base bundle repository (images from Firebase Storage)
   113→        let libraryRepository = BundleLibraryWorkoutsRepository()
   114→        container.register(LibraryWorkoutsRepository.self) { _ in libraryRepository }
   115→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   116→
   117→        // ProgramRepository - carrega programas do bundle
   118→        let programRepository = BundleProgramRepository()
   119→        container.register(ProgramRepository.self) { _ in programRepository }
   120→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   121→
   122→        // ProgramWorkoutRepository - carrega exercícios do catálogo Firestore para programas
   123→        let programWorkoutRepository = FirestoreProgramWorkoutRepository(exerciseService: exerciseService)
   124→        container.register(ProgramWorkoutRepository.self) { _ in programWorkoutRepository }
   125→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   126→
   127→        // ProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepository - salva customizações do usuário (ordem, exclusões)
   128→        let customizationRepository = UserDefaultsProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepository()
   129→        container.register(ProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepositoryProtocol.self) { _ in customizationRepository }
   130→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   131→
   132→        // LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase - carrega treinos de programa com exercícios do catálogo
   133→        container.register(LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase.self) { resolver in
   134→            LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase(
   135→                programRepository: resolver.resolve(ProgramRepository.self)!,
   136→                workoutRepository: resolver.resolve(ProgramWorkoutRepository.self)!
   137→            )
   138→        }
   139→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   140→
   141→        // Workout Composition - New simplified OpenAI stack
   142→        // Enhanced local composer with variation validation
   143→        let historyRepo = container.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   144→        let enhancedLocalComposer = EnhancedLocalWorkoutPlanComposer(
   145→            historyRepository: historyRepo
   146→        )
   147→        container.register(EnhancedLocalWorkoutPlanComposer.self) { _ in enhancedLocalComposer }
   148→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   149→
   150→        // WorkoutPlanComposing: AI via Firebase Functions proxy (key lives on server)
   151→        // Local fallback is used inside the composer on network/AI errors
   152→        container.register(WorkoutPlanComposing.self) { resolver in
   153→            let historyRepository = resolver.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   154→            return NewOpenAIWorkoutComposer(
   155→                client: NewOpenAIClient(),
   156→                localFallback: enhancedLocalComposer,
   157→                historyRepository: historyRepository
   158→            )
   159→        }
   160→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   161→        
   162→        // HealthKit (iPhone) - serviço de integração (PRO gating é feito na UI/fluxos)
   163→        let healthKitService = HealthKitService()
   164→        container.register(HealthKitServicing.self) { _ in healthKitService }
   165→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   166→
   167→        // PRO-72: HealthDataService for body mass / weight data
   168→        container.register(HealthDataServicing.self) { _ in HealthDataService() }
   169→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   170→
   171→        // Note: HealthKitHistorySyncService is registered later after SyncWorkoutCompletionUseCase is available
   172→
   173→        // HealthKit Workout Sync Use Case - auto-exports workouts and imports calories
   174→        container.register(SyncWorkoutWithHealthKitUseCase.self) { resolver in
   175→            SyncWorkoutWithHealthKitUseCase(
   176→                healthKitService: resolver.resolve(HealthKitServicing.self)!,
   177→                historyRepository: resolver.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   178→            )
   179→        }
   180→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   181→
   182→        // Chat Repository (persistence)
   183→        container.register(ChatRepository.self) { _ in
   184→            SwiftDataChatRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
   185→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
   186→
   187→        // AI Chat Service (FitOrb) — resolved lazily inside AIChatViewModel.
   188→        // Not registered here because the OpenAI API key may not exist at launch.
   189→        // The ViewModel creates AIChatService on-demand when a key is available.
   190→
   191→        // Firebase Analytics Service
   192→        let analyticsService = FirebaseAnalyticsService()
   193→        container.register(AnalyticsTracking.self) { _ in analyticsService }
   194→            .inObjectScope(.container)

   672→    private static func makeModelContainer() -> ModelContainer {
   673→        let schema = Schema([
   674→            SDUserProfile.self,
   675→            SDWorkoutHistoryEntry.self,
   676→            SDProEntitlementSnapshot.self,
   677→            SDCachedWorkout.self,
   678→            SDUserStats.self,
   679→            SDCustomWorkoutTemplate.self,
   680→            SDCustomWorkoutCompletion.self,
   681→            SDSavedRoutine.self,
   682→            SDChatMessage.self
   683→        ])
   684→        
   685→        // Configuração com migração automática leve
   686→        let configuration = ModelConfiguration(
   687→            schema: schema,
   688→            isStoredInMemoryOnly: false,
   689→            allowsSave: true
   690→        )
   691→        
   692→        do {
   693→            let container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: [configuration])
   694→            #if DEBUG
   695→            print("[SwiftData] ✅ ModelContainer criado com sucesso")
   696→            #endif
   697→            return container
   698→        } catch {
   699→            // Em caso de erro de migração, logar mas NÃO apagar os dados
   700→            // O usuário deve limpar manualmente se necessário
   701→            #if DEBUG
   702→            print("❌ [SwiftData] Erro ao criar container: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   703→            print("❌ [SwiftData] Se persistir, limpe os dados do app manualmente")
   704→            #endif
   705→            fatalError("Could not create ModelContainer: \(error)")
   706→        }
   707→    }
   708→}

     2→//  UpdateUserStatsUseCase.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 20/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→/// Use case for updating user statistics after workout completion.
    11→struct UpdateUserStatsUseCase: Sendable {
    12→    private let historyRepository: WorkoutHistoryRepository
    13→    private let statsRepository: UserStatsRepository
    14→    private let calculator: UserStatsCalculating
    15→
    16→    init(
    17→        historyRepository: WorkoutHistoryRepository,
    18→        statsRepository: UserStatsRepository,
    19→        calculator: UserStatsCalculating = UserStatsCalculator()
    20→    ) {
    21→        self.historyRepository = historyRepository
    22→        self.statsRepository = statsRepository
    23→        self.calculator = calculator
    24→    }
    25→
    26→    /// Updates user statistics based on workout history.
    27→    /// - Returns: The updated UserStats.
    28→    @discardableResult
    29→    func execute() async throws -> UserStats {
    30→        // Fetch workout history (limit to recent entries for performance)
    31→        let history = try await historyRepository.listEntries()
    32→
    33→        // Get current stats (for longest streak tracking)
    34→        let currentStats = try await statsRepository.getCurrentStats()
    35→
    36→        // Calculate new stats
    37→        let newStats = calculator.computeStats(from: history, currentStats: currentStats)
    38→
    39→        // Persist updated stats
    40→        try await statsRepository.saveStats(newStats)
    41→
    42→        #if DEBUG
    43→        print("[UserStats] Updated: streak=\(newStats.currentStreak), week=\(newStats.weekWorkoutsCount), month=\(newStats.monthWorkoutsCount)")
    44→        #endif
    45→
    46→        return newStats
    47→    }
    48→}

1→//
     2→//  FeatureFlag.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Feature flags for remote configuration of features.
     6→//  Used to control feature rollout and A/B testing.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Key
    12→
    13→/// Keys for remote feature flags controlled via Firebase Remote Config.
    14→/// Each key corresponds to a boolean flag that can be toggled remotely.
    15→enum FeatureFlagKey: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - AI Features
    18→
    19→    /// Enables AI-powered workout generation (OpenAI).
    20→    case aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled = "ai_workout_generation_enabled"
    21→
    22→    /// Enables AI exercise substitution during active workout.
    23→    case aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled = "ai_exercise_substitution_enabled"
    24→
    25→    // MARK: - Social Features
    26→
    27→    /// Enables group creation, joining, and management.
    28→    case socialGroupsEnabled = "social_groups_enabled"
    29→
    30→    /// Enables workout challenges within groups.
    31→    case challengesEnabled = "challenges_enabled"
    32→
    33→    /// Enables leaderboard ranking in groups.
    34→    case leaderboardEnabled = "leaderboard_enabled"
    35→
    36→    /// Enables photo check-in for challenge validation.
    37→    case checkInEnabled = "check_in_enabled"
    38→
    39→    /// Enables group streak tracking and display.
    40→    case groupStreaksEnabled = "group_streaks_enabled"
    41→
    42→    /// Enables social feed with workout proof posts.
    43→    case socialFeedEnabled = "social_feed_enabled"
    44→
    45→    // MARK: - Health & Sync
    46→
    47→    /// Enables Apple Health read/write integration.
    48→    case healthKitSyncEnabled = "healthkit_sync_enabled"
    49→
    50→    /// Enables activity stats with Swift Charts.
    51→    case statsChartsEnabled = "stats_charts_enabled"
    52→
    53→    // MARK: - Content
    54→
    55→    /// Enables structured training programs.
    56→    case programsEnabled = "programs_enabled"
    57→
    58→    /// Enables user-created workout templates.
    59→    case customWorkoutsEnabled = "custom_workouts_enabled"
    60→
    61→    /// Enables exercise library/explorer.
    62→    case exerciseLibraryEnabled = "exercise_library_enabled"
    63→
    64→    // MARK: - Personal Trainer
    65→
    66→    /// Enables personal trainer integration features.
    67→    /// When enabled, users can connect with personal trainers via CMS.
    68→    case personalTrainerEnabled = "personal_trainer_enabled"
    69→
    70→    /// Enables synchronization of workouts from CMS.
    71→    /// When enabled, trainer-assigned workouts sync to the user's app.
    72→    case cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = "cms_workout_sync_enabled"
    73→
    74→    /// Enables chat functionality with personal trainers.
    75→    /// When enabled, users can message their assigned trainer.
    76→    case trainerChatEnabled = "trainer_chat_enabled"
    77→
    78→    // MARK: - Monetization & UX
    79→
    80→    /// Enables paywall/Pro upgrade prompts.
    81→    case paywallEnabled = "paywall_enabled"
    82→
    83→    /// Enables App Store review request after workout completion.
    84→    case reviewRequestEnabled = "review_request_enabled"
    85→
    86→    // MARK: - Operational
    87→
    88→    /// Shows maintenance banner and disables write operations.
    89→    case maintenanceModeEnabled = "maintenance_mode_enabled"
    90→
    91→    /// Shows non-dismissable update dialog directing to App Store.
    92→    case forceUpdateEnabled = "force_update_enabled"
    93→
    94→    // MARK: - Display Name
    95→
    96→    /// Human-readable display name for the feature flag.
    97→    var displayName: String {
    98→        switch self {
    99→        // AI
   100→        case .aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled: return "AI Workout Generation"
   101→        case .aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled: return "AI Exercise Substitution"
   102→        // Social
   103→        case .socialGroupsEnabled: return "Social Groups"
   104→        case .challengesEnabled: return "Challenges"
   105→        case .leaderboardEnabled: return "Leaderboard"
   106→        case .checkInEnabled: return "Check-In"
   107→        case .groupStreaksEnabled: return "Group Streaks"
   108→        case .socialFeedEnabled: return "Social Feed"
   109→        // Health
   110→        case .healthKitSyncEnabled: return "Apple Health Sync"
   111→        case .statsChartsEnabled: return "Stats Charts"
   112→        // Content
   113→        case .programsEnabled: return "Training Programs"
   114→        case .customWorkoutsEnabled: return "Custom Workouts"
   115→        case .exerciseLibraryEnabled: return "Exercise Library"
   116→        // Personal Trainer
   117→        case .personalTrainerEnabled: return "Personal Trainer Integration"
   118→        case .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled: return "CMS Workout Sync"
   119→        case .trainerChatEnabled: return "Trainer Chat"
   120→        // Monetization
   121→        case .paywallEnabled: return "Paywall"
   122→        case .reviewRequestEnabled: return "Review Request"
   123→        // Operational
   124→        case .maintenanceModeEnabled: return "Maintenance Mode"
   125→        case .forceUpdateEnabled: return "Force Update"
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    // MARK: - Default Value
   130→
   131→    /// Default value when remote config is unavailable.
   132→    /// Existing features default to `true` (no regression).
   133→    /// Unreleased features default to `false`.
   134→    var defaultValue: Bool {
   135→        switch self {
   136→        // Unreleased features — disabled by default
   137→        case .socialFeedEnabled,
   138→             .personalTrainerEnabled,
   139→             .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled,
   140→             .trainerChatEnabled,
   141→             .maintenanceModeEnabled,
   142→             .forceUpdateEnabled:
   143→            return false
   144→
   145→        // All existing features — enabled by default
   146→        default:
   147→            return true
   148→        }
   149→    }
   150→}
   151→

1→//
     2→//  HomeView.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by AI on 05/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import SwiftUI
     9→import Swinject
    10→
    11→struct HomeView: View {
    12→    @Environment(AppRouter.self) private var router
    13→    @Environment(WorkoutSessionStore.self) private var sessionStore
    14→
    15→    @State private var viewModel: HomeViewModel
    16→
    17→    init(resolver: Resolver?) {
    18→        guard let resolver = resolver else {
    19→            fatalError("Resolver is required for HomeView")
    20→        }
    21→        self._viewModel = State(wrappedValue: HomeViewModel(resolver: resolver))
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    var body: some View {
    25→        ScrollView {
    26→            VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.lg) {
    27→                // Header with greeting
    28→                HomeHeader(
    29→                    greeting: viewModel.greeting,
    30→                    dateFormatted: viewModel.currentDateFormatted,
    31→                    isPro: viewModel.entitlement.isPro,
    32→                    goalBadgeText: viewModel.goalBadgeText,
    33→                    userName: viewModel.userName,
    34→                    userPhotoURL: viewModel.userPhotoURL,
    35→                    onNotificationTap: { router.push(.notifications, on: .home) }
    36→                )
    37→
    38→                // Week streak circles
    39→                WeekStreakRow(
    40→                    completedDays: viewModel.weekCompletedDays,
    41→                    currentStreak: viewModel.streakDays
    42→                )
    43→                .padding(.horizontal)
    44→
    45→                // Daily stats summary
    46→                DailyStatsCard(
    47→                    workoutsThisWeek: viewModel.workoutsThisWeek,
    48→                    weeklyTarget: viewModel.weeklyTarget,
    49→                    caloriesBurned: viewModel.caloriesBurnedFormatted,
    50→                    streakDays: viewModel.streakDays,
    51→                    totalSets: viewModel.totalSetsThisWeek,
    52→                    avgDuration: viewModel.avgDurationMinutes
    53→                )
    54→
    55→                // Personal trainer card (only if flag enabled)
    56→                if viewModel.isPersonalTrainerEnabled {
    57→                    PersonalTrainerHomeCard(
    58→                        state: viewModel.personalTrainerCardState,
    59→                        onDiscoverTap: { router.push(.personalTrainer, on: .home) },
    60→                        onDashboardTap: { router.push(.trainerDashboard, on: .home) },
    61→                        onChatTap: { router.push(.trainerChat, on: .home) },
    62→                        onWorkoutTap: {
    63→                            if let workoutId = viewModel.todayWorkoutId {
    64→                                router.push(.cmsWorkoutDetail(workoutId), on: .home)
    65→                            }
    66→                        }
    67→                    )
    68→                }
    69→
    70→                // Today's workout section
    71→                if let todayWorkout = viewModel.todayWorkout,
    72→                   case .workoutReady = viewModel.journeyState {
    73→                    TodayWorkoutSection(
    74→                        workout: todayWorkout,
    75→                        programName: viewModel.todayProgramName,
    76→                        onStartWorkout: {
    77→                            sessionStore.start(with: todayWorkout.toWorkoutPlan())
    78→                            router.push(.workoutExecution, on: .home)
    79→                        }
    80→                    )
    81→                }
    82→
    83→                // Content based on journey state
    84→                contentForState
    85→            }
    86→            .padding(.bottom, FitTodaySpacing.xl)
    87→        }
    88→        .background(FitTodayColor.background.ignoresSafeArea())
    89→        .navigationBarHidden(true)
    90→        .task {
    91→            viewModel.onAppear()
    92→        }
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    // MARK: - Content for State
    96→
    97→    @ViewBuilder
    98→    private var contentForState: some View {
    99→        switch viewModel.journeyState {
   100→        case .loading:
   101→            loadingView
   102→
   103→        case .noProfile:
   104→            setupProfileCard
   105→
   106→        case .workoutReady:
   107→            EmptyView()
   108→
   109→        case .workoutCompleted:
   110→            workoutCompletedCard
   111→
   112→        case .error(let message):
   113→            errorView(message: message)
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    // MARK: - Loading View
   118→
   119→    private var loadingView: some View {
   120→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   121→            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg)
   122→                .fill(FitTodayColor.surface)
   123→                .frame(height: 120)
   124→                .shimmer()
   125→
   126→            HStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   127→                ForEach(0..<3, id: \.self) { _ in
   128→                    RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.md)
   129→                        .fill(FitTodayColor.surface)
   130→                        .frame(height: 80)
   131→                        .shimmer()
   132→                }
   133→            }
   134→
   135→            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg)
   136→                .fill(FitTodayColor.surface)
   137→                .frame(height: 100)
   138→                .shimmer()
   139→        }
   140→        .padding(.horizontal)
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    // MARK: - Setup Profile Card
   144→
   145→    private var setupProfileCard: some View {
   146→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   147→            Image(systemName: "person.crop.circle.badge.plus")
   148→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   149→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
   150→
   151→            Text("home.setup_profile.title".localized)
   152→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   153→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   154→
   155→            Text("home.setup_profile.subtitle".localized)
   156→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   157→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   158→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   159→
   160→            Button("home.setup_profile.cta".localized) {
   161→                router.push(.onboarding, on: .home)
   162→            }
   163→            .fitPrimaryStyle()
   164→        }
   165→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   166→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   167→        .background(FitTodayColor.surface)
   168→        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg))
   169→        .padding(.horizontal)
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    // MARK: - Workout Completed Card
   173→
   174→    private var workoutCompletedCard: some View {
   175→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   176→            Image(systemName: "checkmark.circle.fill")
   177→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   178→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.success)
   179→
   180→            Text("home.completed.title".localized)
   181→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   182→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   183→
   184→            Text("home.completed.subtitle".localized)
   185→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   186→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   187→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   188→        }
   189→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   190→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   191→        .background(FitTodayColor.success.opacity(0.1))
   192→        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg))
   193→        .overlay(
   194→            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg)
   195→                .stroke(FitTodayColor.success.opacity(0.3), lineWidth: 1)
   196→        )
   197→        .padding(.horizontal)
   198→    }
   199→
   200→    // MARK: - Error View
   201→
   202→    private func errorView(message: String) -> some View {
   203→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   204→            Image(systemName: "exclamationmark.triangle.fill")
   205→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   206→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.error)
   207→
   208→            Text("home.error.title".localized)
   209→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   210→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   211→
   212→            Text(message)
   213→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   214→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   215→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   216→
   217→            Button("common.retry".localized) {
   218→                Task { await viewModel.refresh() }
   219→            }
   220→            .fitSecondaryStyle()
   221→        }
   222→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   223→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   224→        .padding(.horizontal)
   225→    }
   226→}
   227→
   228→// MARK: - Preview
   229→
   230→#Preview {
   231→    let container = Container()
   232→    container.register(UserProfileRepository.self) { _ in MockUserProfileRepository() }
   233→    container.register(EntitlementRepository.self) { _ in MockEntitlementRepository() }
   234→    container.register(ProgramRepository.self) { _ in BundleProgramRepository() }
   235→    container.register(LibraryWorkoutsRepository.self) { _ in
   236→        BundleLibraryWorkoutsRepository()
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    return NavigationStack {
   240→        HomeView(resolver: container)
   241→            .environment(AppRouter())
   242→            .environment(WorkoutSessionStore(resolver: container))
   243→    }
   244→}
   245→
   246→// MARK: - Preview Mocks
   247→
   248→private final class MockUserProfileRepository: UserProfileRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
   249→    func loadProfile() async throws -> UserProfile? {
   250→        UserProfile(
   251→            mainGoal: .hypertrophy,
   252→            availableStructure: .fullGym,
   253→            preferredMethod: .traditional,
   254→            level: .intermediate,
   255→            healthConditions: [.none],
   256→            weeklyFrequency: 4
   257→        )
   258→    }
   259→
   260→    func saveProfile(_ profile: UserProfile) async throws {}
   261→}
   262→
   263→private final class MockEntitlementRepository: EntitlementRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
   264→    func currentEntitlement() async throws -> ProEntitlement { .free }
   265→    func entitlementStream() -> AsyncStream<ProEntitlement> {
   266→        AsyncStream { continuation in continuation.finish() }
   267→    }
   268→}
   269→

# Tasks — Sistema de Streaks & XP com Níveis (PRO-90)

## Summary

| Task | Title                                 | Size | Status  |
| ---- | ------------------------------------- | ---- | ------- |
| 1.0  | Domain Entities + Feature Flag        | S    | pending |
| 2.0  | XP Repository (SwiftData + Firestore) | M    | pending |
| 3.0  | AwardXP Use Case                      | M    | pending |
| 4.0  | Home Screen XP Level Card             | M    | pending |
| 5.0  | Level-Up Celebration View             | M    | pending |
| 6.0  | Workout Completion Integration        | S    | pending |
| 7.0  | Unit Tests                            | M    | pending |

---

# Task 1.0: Domain Entities + Feature Flag (S)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Create the core domain entities for the XP/Level system and add the feature flag.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 1.1 Create `UserXP` struct in `Domain/Entities/UserXP.swift` with `totalXP`, `lastAwardDate`, computed `level`, `currentLevelXP`, `xpToNextLevel`, `levelProgress`, `levelTitle`
- [ ] 1.2 Create `XPLevel` enum in `Domain/Entities/XPLevel.swift` with cases (iniciante, guerreiro, tita, lenda, imortal), `init(level:)`, `icon`, `rawValue` (display name)
- [ ] 1.3 Create `XPTransaction` struct and `XPTransactionType` enum in `Domain/Entities/XPTransaction.swift` with static `xpAmount(for:)` method
- [ ] 1.4 Add `gamificationEnabled` case to `FeatureFlagKey` with default `false`

## Success Criteria

- All entities compile and follow project conventions (@Sendable, Codable)
- Feature flag registered with correct raw value and default
- No force unwraps, proper value types

## Dependencies

- None (foundational task)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Domain/Entities/UserStats.swift` (reference for patterns)
- `FitToday/Domain/Entities/FeatureFlag.swift`
- `FitToday/Domain/Entities/GroupStreakModels.swift` (reference for similar enums)

---

# Task 2.0: XP Repository (SwiftData + Firestore) (M)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Create the repository layer for XP persistence (local SwiftData + remote Firestore).

## Subtasks

- [ ] 2.1 Create `XPRepository` protocol in `Domain/Protocols/XPRepository.swift`
- [ ] 2.2 Create `SDUserXP` SwiftData model in `Data/Models/SDUserXP.swift` (singleton pattern with `id = "current"`)
- [ ] 2.3 Create `FBUserXP` Firestore DTO in `Data/DTOs/FBUserXP.swift`
- [ ] 2.4 Create `SwiftDataXPRepository` in `Data/Repositories/SwiftDataXPRepository.swift` implementing `XPRepository`
- [ ] 2.5 Register `SDUserXP` in the SwiftData ModelContainer (check existing registration pattern)
- [ ] 2.6 Register `XPRepository` in `AppContainer.swift`

## Success Criteria

- Repository reads/writes XP locally via SwiftData
- Firestore sync via existing `FirebaseUserService` (add XP fields to user document)
- Follows existing singleton pattern from `SDUserStats`

## Dependencies

- Task 1.0 (entities)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Data/Models/SDUserStats.swift` (pattern reference)
- `FitToday/Data/Repositories/SwiftDataUserStatsRepository.swift` (pattern reference)
- `FitToday/Presentation/DI/AppContainer.swift`

---

# Task 3.0: AwardXP Use Case (M)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Create the use case that awards XP after workout completion, calculates streak bonuses, and detects level-up.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 3.1 Create `XPAwardResult` struct with `previousLevel`, `newLevel`, `xpAwarded`, `totalXP`, `didLevelUp`
- [ ] 3.2 Create `AwardXPUseCase` in `Domain/UseCases/AwardXPUseCase.swift`
- [ ] 3.3 Implement `execute(type:currentStreak:)` method with XP calculation + streak bonus logic
- [ ] 3.4 Register `AwardXPUseCase` in `AppContainer.swift`

## Success Criteria

- Awards correct XP per transaction type (100 workout, 200 streak-7d, 500 streak-30d, 500 challenge)
- Correctly detects level-up (level before != level after)
- Returns complete `XPAwardResult`
- Pure business logic, no UI dependencies

## Dependencies

- Task 2.0 (repository)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Domain/UseCases/UpdateUserStatsUseCase.swift` (pattern reference)

---

# Task 4.0: Home Screen XP Level Card (M)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Create the XP/Level display component for the home screen and integrate with HomeViewModel.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 4.1 Create `XPLevelCard` view in `Presentation/Features/Home/Components/XPLevelCard.swift`
- [ ] 4.2 Add `userXP: UserXP?` and `isGamificationEnabled: Bool` to `HomeViewModel`
- [ ] 4.3 Load XP data in `HomeViewModel.loadUserData()` (gated by feature flag)
- [ ] 4.4 Add `XPLevelCard` to `HomeView` body after `WeekStreakRow` (gated by flag)

## Success Criteria

- Shows level number, nome temático, SF Symbol icon
- Progress bar with XP current / 1000
- Animates progress bar smoothly
- Hidden when `gamification_enabled` flag is off
- Follows FitToday design system (FitTodayColor, FitTodayFont, FitTodaySpacing)

## Dependencies

- Task 1.0 (entities)
- Task 2.0 (repository)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/HomeView.swift`
- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/HomeViewModel.swift`
- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/Components/WeekStreakRow.swift` (style reference)
- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/Components/DailyStatsCard.swift` (style reference)

---

# Task 5.0: Level-Up Celebration View (M)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Create the confetti celebration overlay for level-up moments during workout completion.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 5.1 Create `ConfettiView` using Canvas + TimelineView for particle animation
- [ ] 5.2 Create `LevelUpCelebrationView` overlay with new level, nome temático, confetti
- [ ] 5.3 Add auto-dismiss after 5s or on tap
- [ ] 5.4 Respect `accessibilityReduceMotion` (skip animation, show static)

## Success Criteria

- Confetti animation is smooth (60fps)
- Shows new level number + nome temático + icon
- Dismisses on tap or after 5s timeout
- Respects reduce motion accessibility setting
- Follows FitToday design system

## Dependencies

- Task 1.0 (entities for XPLevel display)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Workout/WorkoutCompletionView.swift` (integration target)

---

# Task 6.0: Workout Completion Integration (S)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Wire the XP award system into the existing workout completion flow.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 6.1 Add `AwardXPUseCase` dependency to `WorkoutCompletionView`
- [ ] 6.2 Call `AwardXPUseCase.execute()` after `UpdateUserStatsUseCase` in the completion task
- [ ] 6.3 Gate XP award behind `gamification_enabled` feature flag
- [ ] 6.4 Show `LevelUpCelebrationView` overlay when `didLevelUp == true`
- [ ] 6.5 Show XP earned summary in completion view ("+100 XP")

## Success Criteria

- XP awarded after every workout completion (when flag is on)
- Level-up celebration displays correctly
- Existing completion flow is not broken
- No performance regression (< 100ms added)

## Dependencies

- Task 3.0 (use case)
- Task 5.0 (celebration view)

## Relevant Files

- `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Workout/WorkoutCompletionView.swift`
- `FitToday/Domain/UseCases/UpdateUserStatsUseCase.swift`

---

# Task 7.0: Unit Tests (M)

<critical>Read the prd.md and techspec.md files in this folder.</critical>

## Overview

Write unit tests for all business logic: entities, use case, and repository.

## Subtasks

- [ ] 7.1 `UserXPTests` — test level calculation, progress, title mapping for various XP amounts
- [ ] 7.2 `XPLevelTests` — test all level ranges and icons
- [ ] 7.3 `AwardXPUseCaseTests` — test workout XP award, streak-7 bonus, streak-30 bonus, challenge bonus, level-up detection
- [ ] 7.4 `XPTransactionTests` — test `xpAmount(for:)` for all types

## Success Criteria

- 80%+ coverage on `UserXP`, `XPLevel`, `AwardXPUseCase`
- Tests use mocks/stubs for repository (follow existing `MockFeedRepository` pattern)
- All tests pass

## Dependencies

- Task 1.0, 2.0, 3.0

## Relevant Files

- `FitTodayTests/Presentation/Features/SocialFeed/SocialFeedViewModelTests.swift` (mock pattern reference)

    11→    let onDismiss: () -> Void
    12→
    13→    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) private var reduceMotion
    14→    @State private var showContent = false
    15→
    16→    var body: some View {

    21→

    35→                    .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)

    39→                    .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
    40→

    43→                    .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
    44→            }
    45→            .scaleEffect(showContent ? 1 : 0.5)
    46→            .opacity(showContent ? 1 : 0)
    47→        }
    48→        .onAppear {
    49→            withAnimation(reduceMotion ? .none : .spring(response: 0.5, dampingFraction: 0.6)) {
    50→                showContent = true
    51→            }
    52→        }
    53→        .task {
    54→            try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(5))
    55→            onDismiss()
    56→        }
    57→    }
    58→}
    59→
    60→// MARK: - Confetti View
    61→
    62→struct ConfettiView: View {
    63→    @State private var particles: [ConfettiParticle] = []
    64→    @State private var startTime = Date()
    65→
    66→    private let colors: [Color] = [.red, .orange, .yellow, .green, .blue, .purple, .pink]
    67→
    68→    var body: some View {
    69→        TimelineView(.animation) { timeline in
    70→            Canvas { context, size in
    71→                let elapsed = timeline.date.timeIntervalSince(startTime)
    72→                for particle in particles {
    73→                    let age = elapsed - particle.delay
    74→                    guard age > 0 else { continue }
    75→
    76→                    let x = particle.startX * size.width + sin(age * particle.wobble) * 30
    77→                    let y = age * particle.speed * size.height / 4
    78→                    let opacity = max(0, 1 - age / 4)
    79→
    80→                    guard y < size.height, opacity > 0 else { continue }
    81→
    82→                    var path = Path()
    83→                    path.addRect(CGRect(x: x - 4, y: y - 4, width: 8, height: 8))
    84→                    context.fill(path, with: .color(particle.color.opacity(opacity)))
    85→                }
    86→            }
    87→        }
    88→        .onAppear {

    91→                    startX: Double.random(in: 0...1),
    92→                    speed: Double.random(in: 0.5...1.5),
    93→                    delay: Double.random(in: 0...0.5),
    94→                    wobble: Double.random(in: 2...6),
    95→                    color: colors.randomElement() ?? .yellow
    96→                )
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→    }
   100→}

gh pr create --repo Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday --base main --head feat/pro-90-xp-levels-system --title "feat(gamification): add XP/Level system (PRO-90)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **Domain entities**: `UserXP`, `XPLevel`, `XPTransaction` with level calculation (1000 XP = 1 level), thematic names (Iniciante → Guerreiro → Titã → Lenda → Imortal)
- **Repository layer**: `XPRepository` protocol + `SwiftDataXPRepository` with singleton pattern (`id = "current"`) + `FBUserXP` DTO for future Firestore sync
- **Use case**: `AwardXPUseCase` with streak bonus logic (7d: +200 XP, 30d: +500 XP)
- **Home screen**: `XPLevelCard` showing level, thematic name, animated progress bar, and XP counter
- **Level-up celebration**: `LevelUpCelebrationView` overlay with confetti, auto-dismiss after 5s, respects `accessibilityReduceMotion`
- **Workout integration**: XP awarded after workout completion, gated by `gamification_enabled` feature flag
- **Feature flag**: `gamification_enabled` (default: `false`) in Firebase Remote Config
- **Unit tests**: `UserXPTests`, `XPLevelTests`, `XPTransactionTests`, `AwardXPUseCaseTests`

## Test plan

- [ ] Enable `gamification_enabled` in Firebase Remote Config
- [ ] Complete a workout → verify +100 XP awarded and shown in completion view
- [ ] Check home screen shows XP Level Card with correct level and progress bar
- [ ] Accumulate XP to cross 1000 threshold → verify level-up celebration plays
- [ ] Verify streak bonus: 7+ day streak gives +200 XP, 30+ day streak gives +500 XP
- [ ] Disable feature flag → verify no XP components visible anywhere
- [ ] Verify `accessibilityReduceMotion` skips confetti animation

Closes PRO-90

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
**Feature-marker workflow complete for PRO-90.**

Here's a summary of everything implemented:

### PRO-90: Sistema de Streaks & XP com Niveis

**New files created (13):**
- `Domain/Entities/UserXP.swift` — XP entity with level calculation (1000 XP = 1 level)
- `Domain/Entities/XPLevel.swift` — Thematic levels enum (Iniciante → Guerreiro → Tita → Lenda → Imortal)
- `Domain/Entities/XPTransaction.swift` — Transaction types and XP amounts
- `Domain/Protocols/XPRepository.swift` — Repository protocol
- `Data/Models/SDUserXP.swift` — SwiftData model (singleton pattern)
- `Data/DTOs/FBUserXP.swift` — Firestore DTO for future sync
- `Data/Repositories/SwiftDataXPRepository.swift` — SwiftData implementation
- `Domain/UseCases/AwardXPUseCase.swift` — XP award + streak bonus + level-up detection
- `Presentation/Features/Home/Components/XPLevelCard.swift` — Home screen card with animated progress bar
- `Presentation/Features/Workout/LevelUpCelebrationView.swift` — Confetti celebration overlay
- 4 test files: `UserXPTests`, `XPLevelTests`, `XPTransactionTests`, `AwardXPUseCaseTests`

**Modified files (5):**
- `FeatureFlag.swift` — Added `gamificationEnabled` case
- `AppContainer.swift` — Registered `SDUserXP`, `XPRepository`, `AwardXPUseCase`
- `HomeViewModel.swift` — Added XP loading gated by feature flag
- `HomeView.swift` — Added `XPLevelCard` after `WeekStreakRow`
- `WorkoutCompletionView.swift` — Added XP award call + "+100 XP" badge + level-up overlay

**PR**: https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/pull/104
